inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1912.)
ŚŌḺAPURAM, VELLORE TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On two detached stones lying in the compound of a ruined Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
Fragment. In characters of the tenth century A.D. Seems to record some land
grant (90 kuḷi ?) by [Kannara]dēvar alias Pṛithivigaṅgaraiyaṉ. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. 346 of 1912.)
KŌVILŪR, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Śiva temple.

p.b:
King: Vīrapratāpa Prauḍha-Dēvarāya Year: Śaka 1377, Yuva, Karkaṭka,
alias Mallikārjunarāya. śu. di. 7, Monday, Sōdi = 21st July 1455 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. It records that Timmayanāyakkar, son of Mallayanāyakkar,
donated a village called Gaṇapatimaḍu in Maṅgala-nāḍu, near the hill Rājagambhīraṉ-
malai, for conducting worship to the god, Tirumālar-nāyiṉār, for the victory and all
around prosperity to the king, Mallikārjuṉararāyar.

l.ta:
kācciṉairāyar piṟutiviirācciyam paṇṇa niṉṟa cakāttam āyirattu
muṉ-

ṉūṟṟu eḻupatteḻiṉ melcell[ā*] niṉṟa yuvavaruṣattu kaṟka[ṭa]ka
nāyaṟṟu pū-
• ṭu • ta aḷavukku itta[r]mmattukku akitam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkai

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1912.)
KŌVILŪR, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Śiva temple.

p.b:
King: Vīrapratāpa Dēvarāya Year: Śaka 1353, virōdhikiṟi, Makara, śu. Full Moon,
Thursday Pūśam, lunar eclipse = January 17, 1432 A.D.

inscription.p:
It records a gift of a village Bālavārai at Padiēripaṟṟu, near Rājagambhīran
(division), to the god Tirumūlanayiṉār in Maṅgala-nāḍu, on the auspicious occasion
of lunar eclipse for burning a lamp and providing food offering onetime (oru-sandhi)
by Aṇṇāmaḷaināthar, son of Nārasiṅgadēvar an official of Malaipaṟṟu. It is recorded
that this grant was made for the victory and glory of the king, Dēvarāya mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. 348 of 1912.)
KŌVILŪR, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the slab set up infront of the Śiva temple.
inscription.p:
A warrior is portrayed holding a bow while an animal (tiger) is facing him. It
is damaged. Records the death of a certain Mahāvalli, in a tiger hunt, probably at
Maṅgalapuram.

This inscription on the grounds of palaeography may be assigned to the
13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. 349 of 1912.)
VĪRALŪR, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On a slab set up near the Pūṅgāvanattammaṉ temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged and the language is also faulty. It is engraved in peculiar characters
of about the tenth century A.D. It mentions various mercantile bodies tisai-āyiratt-
aiññūṟṟuvar, nānādēśi and vīrasauravi, and seems to refer to the conversion of a village
(Vākūr ?) to vīrattāvaḷam. It also appears to fix duties on certain communities. The
record also refers to the above mercantile commodities of Sakku-maṅgalam. Other
details are not clear.

l.ta: ḻu nūṟṟukkātam māṟṟuviṭṭār pi • makac cey-

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. 350 of 1912.)
VĪRALŪR, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On a slab erected near the Gaṇēsa temple.

p.b: King: Dēvarāyamahārāya II. Year: Kīlaka ie., Śaka 1351.

inscription.p:
It records that two individuals, Vidiyār Nāyakkar and Viṭṭappa Nāyakkar visited
Viśaiyāndaram (Vijayanagaram) and obtained from (the king) four hamlets,
Terindamutti, Śiṟupākkam, Taṇḍaṉapaṭṭi and Nilaiyiṭṭāṉ-ēndal, situated on the north
bank of Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu, as sarvamānya-iṟaiyili to the god, Aruḷāḷanāthar and goddess
Uṇṇāmalai-nāchchiyār and also obtained a charity-deed, during the regime of
Deṇṇāyaka.

Further it is also stated that as these villages had dry lands and did not have
wet lands to cultivate, they (temple authorities) were permitted to dig wells and excavate
tanks to create wet lands and settle people which enable them to cultivate various
crops.

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. 351 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west and south walls, central shrine, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and Damaged. Records that Rāyappaḍaiyar of Koṇḍappaḷḷi and the
ūrārs of Kuḷappākkam and other ūr granted land to the god, Tirumalar-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār,
of Dēvikāpuram as iṟaiyili-dēvadānam.

l.ta:
yarum murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu ku[ḷap]pākkam ūravar kaḷappāḷanpaṟṟu
irāyarcempiyataraiyar maruṅkuṭaiyār uḷḷa

ūravarom taṉmacātaṉam paṇṇiṉapaṭi [tevakāpuṟam] tirumalar
uṭaiya nāyanāṟ ••••••ma

[ṉa]m āka viṭṭom vaṉaḷa atta inta taram canti[ṟā]tittayava[rai]y
••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. 352 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadēvarāya. Year: Śaka 1442, Bahudānya,
Makara, śu. di. 5. Friday, Hasta = January 21, 1519 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that the pontiff of Bhīkshā-maḍam Īśānaśivachāriyār, who was also
responsible for the treasury of Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkapuram,
tāṉattār (trustees), māyēśvarars (devotees), and kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigaḷ (weaver merchants)
conferred the kāṇipaṟṟu rights on Tirumalai-Nāyakkar, son of Eṭṭappa-nāyakkar, the
Kaṉṉaḍiya-nāyakkapāḍikal, residing at Marudarasar-paḍaivīḍu, three plots of land in
two villages totalling 700 kuḻi of land, of the temple and also one old house at
Dēvakkapuram. This inscription also stipulates that he could induct cultivators (kuḍiy-
ēṟṟi) and have them rise any crops he liked on the land which included both wet
and dry lands. The land was also authorised to be irrigated by the (local) tank and
taxes such as kaḍamai, kāṇikkai, poṉvaṟkam, palatāṉiyavaṟkam, maravaḍai, māvaḍai,
vāśalkaḍamai, peṟkaḍamai, taṟikaḍamai, and other taxes were to be remitted to the
treasury. The inscription further informs us that the lands thus leased to the donee
as maṉaiāṅkaṭṭu-kuttagai, with the condition, that he would have to remit 50 paṇam
of money and 50 kalam of paddy as kandāyam to the treasury of Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanayiṉār temple.

The pontiff of Bhīkshā-maḍam, Chandranambi, the tāṉattār, the temple
accountant Īśānabrahmarāyaṉ alias Eṭṭi Appaṉ Uttaṇḍi and others figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ra metiṉi micura kaṇṭa
kaṭṭāri cāḷuva kiṟuṣṇa(ka) teva
amara pakṣattu pañcamiyū cukkiṟavāramum peṟṟa a

nā[ṭ*]ṭu muruka maṅkalapaṟṟu irācakempīraṉmalaikku aṭuttappaṟṟu

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. 353 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadēvarāya Year: Śaka 1441, Bahudānya,
Karkaṭka śu. di. 13, Asvini Siddhayōga = January 7, 1519 A.D.

inscription.p:
It records the conferment of the right of cultivation (uḻavu-kāṇiāṭchi) of a village
owned by the temple (tirunāmakkāṇi) on a certain Sadāśiva Nāyakkar, son of Mallappar
Nāyakkar, a Kaṉṉaḍiya nāyakka and a resident of Marudarasar-paḍaivīḍu, by the
pontiff of Bhīkshā-maṭha of Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple, tāṉattār (trustees),
māyēsvarar (temple servants) of the Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkāpuram
at Murgamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟak-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of
Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍalam. It is recorded that the tank of the said village was breached
and the lands fell fallow. Sadāśiva Nāyakkar was given the right to repair the tank,
reclaim all the lands and settle people. Further the record mentions that he was given
not only the right of cultivation but also to cultivate any plants of his choice, but
was required to remit taxes such as vāsalpēṟ-kaḍamai, taṟikkaḍamai, sekkōṭṭu,
talaiyārikkam, nallerudu, and other taxes as kandāyam at the temple treasury,
commencing from the month of Āḍi in the cyclic year Vegutāṉiya. Further he was
also given a house at Dēvakkāpuram as uḻavu-kāṇiaṭchi. Īśanaśivāchāriyār, the pontiff,
Karaiēraviṭṭar Chandranambi, the tāṉattār and several other figure as signatories.

l.ta:
hāmaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri cāḷuva
kiṣṇateva

mel cellā niṉṟa vekutāṉiyavaruṣam kaṟkaṭakanāyaṟṟu
pūṟuva pakṣattu

ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟa koṭṭattu mel kuṉṟa
nāṭu murukamaṅkalapaṟṟu irācakem[pī]raṉmalaikku aṭuttappa[ṟṟu]

ṉam tevakk[ā]puram nayiṉār
tirumalaruṭaiya nayiṉār śrīpaṇṭārattukku kaṭavāṉ pikṣāmaṭam
yīcāṉacivācāriya»

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. 354 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Immaḍirāyadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1429. Raktākshi,
Mīṉa, ba. 10. Friday, irregular. But tallies with 1505 A.D. February 28.
Both the cyclic year, Raktākshi and Śaka year 1429 are wrong. The cyclic
year should be Krōdhana and Śaka year 1427.

inscription.p:
It records that the pontiff of Bhīkshā-maḍam, Viśvēśvara Śivāchārya Aiyyaṉ, who
was also responsible for the treasury of the Tirumalar-uḍaiyār temple at Dēvikkāpuram,
a dēvasthānam in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam
(a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam and the tāṉattār of the temple conferred
the right of cultivation, uḻavukāṇiyāṭchi in Maṇaṟpākkam alias Sōmāśipuram at
Kaṉṉaḍiya Māraṇai, a obaḷi (a division) in Śīrmaipuram on a certain Nayiṉān piḷḷai,
son of Kaḷappāḷar Sūryadēvaṉār, a shepherd of Dēvikkāpuram. It is also stated that
Āḷappirandāṉ Samarapuṅgava-dīkshitar of Āgni-gōtrā, who was granted lands in the
same village on an earlier occasion by Tirumalai-Nāyakkar and Īśvara-nāyakkar, gave
the land back, after retaining five hundred kuḻi of land in the same village for himself,
for providing oblations during early morning service to the god of Dēvikkāpuram,
and this piece of land was given to Nayiṉār piḷḷai. It is also mentioned that latter
was to bring the cultivators, dig wells and tank and reclaim the land and was to get
the land cultivated with the crops of his choice. He was not to remit taxes kaḍamai,
kāṇikkai, poṉvari, māvaḍai, maravaḍai, vāḻai, karumbu, iñji, and also vāśaṟkaḍamai,
pēṟkaḍamai, taṟikkaḍamai, sekkōṭṭu-kkāṇikkai, kaṭṭāyam, mādārikkam, talaiyārikkam,
āsuvakkaḍamai, paṭṭaḍai, nūlāyam, paḻavari, puduvari, naṟpaśu, nallerumai, naṟkiḍā, and
several other taxes, but instead the kāṇi holder was to remit certain amount of paddy
and money thrice in a year to the temple treaury. It is also stated further that he
was to enjoy this right of cultivation (uḻavaḍai-kāṇiyāṭchchi) hereditarily.

Viśvēśvarasivāchārya, Samarapuṅgava-dīkshitar, Tirumalainambi, and others,
including the accountant of the temple at Dēvakkapuram, Īśurabrahmarāyaṉ Eṭṭi Appaṉ,
figure as signatories.

miṉanāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu cukkiṟavāramum tecamiyum peṟṟa uttirattu

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. 355 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: ⎯⎯ Year: Śaka 1422. Rautri, Kaṟkaṭaka, śu. di. 7. Friday, Hasta = July 3, 1500 A.D.

inscription.p:
It records a gift of 500 kuḻi of wet land to the east of a lake at Sembiyamaṅgalam,
a village owned by the temple (tirunāmattilkāṇi), a housesite at Dēvikkāpuram and
also four nāḻi of rice daily as offerings, (praśādam) to two Gurukkaḷ Kāḷattināthar
and Maṟāuaḷḷattirundār, who recite song (pāṭṭu) before the god of the Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkapuram, a paṟṟu (territorial-division) in Palkuṉṟa-
kkōṭṭam in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷa-
maṇḍalam, by Tirumalai-Nāyakkar and Īśvara-nāyakkar, sons of Eṭṭappa-nāyakkar, for
the merit of Narasa-Nāyakka. It was stipulated that atleast one person should recite
the song (before the deity).

l.ta:
ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟakoṭṭattu melkuṉṟa
nāṭṭu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu

tevakkāpuram uṭaiyārtirumalaruṭaiyanayiṉārkku tiruppāṭṭu otukiṟa
kurukkaḷ kiṭṭacceri uṭaiyār pāramānatevanayiṉār

makaṉāṟ kāḷattinātar(ṟ)kum meṟpaṭi maṟāuḷḷattiruntārkkum
ivviruvarum tirumalar uṭaiya nayiṉāṟku tiruppāṭṭu oti tevakkāpuram

iṉnayiṉār tirunāmattil kāṇi cempiyamaṅkalam ēri kiḻ nañcai
nilattile añṉuṟu

maṉaiyum peṟṟu nāḷ oṉṟukku nāṉāḻi aricipiṟacātamumpeṟṟu
cantiṟā

iraṇṭu perile oruttar tiruppāṭṭuoti[k*]koṇṭu cukattile
irukka kaṭava(ā)tākavum inta taṉmattukku yātoruttar

ṉavarkaḷ keṅkaikkaraiyile kārām pacuvaikkoṉṟa
pāvattile poka kaṭaivarkaḷākavuṟ

taṉmamāka e[t*]tappanāyakkar kumārar tirumalaināyakkaru[m] īcura

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. 356 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achyutayyadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1455, Vikṛiti, siṁha, śu. 10.
Friday, uttiram. August 29, 1533 A.D. but the cyclic year was Vijaya not Vikṛiti.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that Īśānaśiva-chāriyār of Bhīkshā-maṭha, who was also
bound for service in the Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkapuram, a territory
(paṟṟu) situated next to Rājagambīran-malai in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam
(a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, temple official, and kaikkōḷa-mudaligaḷ
conferred the cultivating right (kāṇiyāṭchi) on Kāḷattīsura-Nāyakkar, son of Tirumalai-
Nāyakkar, the Kaṉṉaḍiya-nāyakamārs residing at Marudarasar-paḍaivīḍu. Accordingly,
he was given the privilege of managing the above temple affairs as secretary, supervisor
and chief executive officer. It is also stated that for these duties, he was given all
wet and dry lands in the village and another 1000 kuḻi of wet land at the village,
Kailāyam. Besides these he was also entitled to receive daily one kuṟuṇi of rice, five
dishes, one palam of sandal and also betel leaves and arecanut as prasādam from
temple. He was also to receive ten paṇam of money and ten kalam of paddy for
employing an accountant under him.

The temple accountant Uttaṇḍar, the pontiff, Īśānaśiva-chāriyār, and the tāṉattār,
Karaiēṟaviṭṭār Chandranambi and several others figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecura metiṉimicura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri
cāḷuva ariyarāya vipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ
muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭukoṇṭu koṇṭanāṭukuṭātāṉ

uṭaiyar kumārar accutaiyyateva

ceyyāṟṟukku teṉ pirivāṉa

āka inta vakaippaṭi cuvantiram uṇṭāṉatellām kāṇiyāṭci āka

• • kāliṅkarāyar kollāviṭṭār vāṇavarāyar uḷḷiṭṭa ūravar

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. 357 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: ⎯⎯ Year: Śaka 1425, Rudhirōdgarin Mṛigasīrisha śu. 2.

inscription.p: Impression of this inscription is missing and only the transcript is available.

Records that Tirumalai-Nāyakkar, Īśvara-Nāyakkar, Viśvēśvaraśivāchārya, and
administrator of the temple gave a deed (kaiyyōlai) at the temple in Dēvakkapuram
to Samarapuṅgava-Dīkshitar of Ātrēya-gōtra and Āpasthambha-sūtra, conveying the gift
of seven hundred kuḻi of dry land, sixty kuḻi (of wet land ?), a house measuring sixty
feet with all appertenances at the village, Kailāsam, for the merit of the king (svāmi)
Narasa-Nāyaka, who attained Śivalōkaprāpti (died).

edition.l:
kradhamyakaḷukkum yoma[ru]māka kaṭavatākavum [ svadaṉtiṟa] vi[śa]ṇa
puṇ[ṇya]

m yādat taru pālanam pāda •pāhāyoṇasa • m •[ palam] bhavēt
dānapāla

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. 358 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadēvamahārāya Year: Śaka 1441, Bahudhānya, Kaṟkaṭaka śu. 13, Tuesday
Aśvini = 20 July, 1518 A.D. But the Śaka era is 1440; star is Pūraṭṭadi.

inscription.p:
This inscription is clumsily engraved and records that Harihara Nāyakkaṉ, son
of Mallappa-Nāyakkar, the Kaṉṉaḍiya nāyakka-pāḍigal who resided at Marudarasarpaḍaivīdu
had endowed (the tax) 33(1/2) paṇam to the temple of Tirumalar-uḍaiya-nāyiṉār at
Dēvikkāpuram. This money was the tax levied on the shepherds of Kāṅgēyankuppam,
which was owned by Harihara Nāyakaṇ. This gift was made for the merit of
Kṛishṇadēvarāya and Tirumalai Nāyakkar on the occasion of the lunar eclipse which
occurred on the 27th day of Vaikāsi in the cyclic year of Vegutāṉiya.

The names of the shepherds (maṉṟāḍiyaḷ) and the amount of tax remitted by
each of them, is given.

Harihara Nāyakka, figures as a signatory, while the temple accountant Uttaṇḍi,
son of Īśura Mādarasan Nāyaṉ Appar, wrote this record.

l.ta:
niyum

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall of the first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. It records a gift of land, including two wells, as kāṇiyāttchi
in Murugamaṅgalam by Tirumalai-Nāyakkar and Īśura-Nāyakkar to the Tirumalāruḍaiyār
temple at Dēvakkāpuram in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub division of) Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-
maṇḍalam, for providing incence during the twilight service (vandikāppu) and on other
auspicious days. Also provides for the anointment of the deity and for the paste
(tiruchchāndu).

edition.l: ariciyil a(m)mutum ( cantrātittava)

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Badly engraved. This inscription records that Nayaṉappa Nāyakkar, an agent of
the official (aḍaippa) Mallappa-Nāyakkar, assign certain persons to provide ghee for
conducting rituals on the occasion of tiruvādirai to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār
at Dēvakkapuram, for the merit of Amalappa-Nāyakkar.

The temple accountant figures as signatory. It further records that this was the
charity of Chellapperumāḷ piḷḷai.

l.ta: lar uṭaiyanayiṉāṟku tiruvātiraitirunāḷukku neyi ā[ṭi] aru-

[ḷukai]yil karai[yi]rāyapāṭi cevvaṇman taṭi aḷakkum cūlattāl
neyikku

inta neyi tiruvātirai
tirunāḷukku aḷakkakaṭaivar ākavu

keṅkai karaiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa toṣattilepoka
kaṭaivar ākavu

maikku koyilkaṇakkupiḷḷai eḻuttu § cellapperumāḷpiḷḷai
taṉmam § tiruviti • cevvaṇṇa

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. 361 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadēvarāya Year: Śaka 1443, Vikrama,
Kārttigai, Ekādasi, Monday = 9th July 1520 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that Sadāsivanāyakkar appointed Arayavaṟai Pachchaiyaṉ, son of
Narāyaṇaṉ Kōn, as supplier of oil (tiruviḷakkuḍi), for the merit of Lord (svāmi)
Tirumalai-Nāyakkar, inorder to supply oil annually on the occasion of Kāṟtigai to
the temple of Tirumalar-uḍaiyanāyaṉār at Dēvikkāppuram in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu
in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍi and also the temple trustee (tānam), Māyēśurar
Mudaliyār figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ekāteciyum comavārattu nāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu
palkuṉṟak[oṭ]-

ṭattu melkuṉṟanāṭu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu tevakkāpuram tirumalar
uṭaiyanayiṉāṟku
yaṇaṉ kon makaṉ arayavaṟai paccaiya[ṉ] varuṣa

u uttaṇṭi eḻuttu itānam m[ā]yecurar
mutaliyār eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. 362 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: Kṛishṇadeva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1444, Vṛisha, Friday.

inscription.p:
Badly engraved. Records that a lady Dēvayiri Ammaṉ, daughter of Dēvappuḍaiyār
of Marudaraspaḍaivīḍu, made a gift of lamp to the god Tirumalari-uḍaiyanayinār in
Dēvakkāpuram, a dēvasthāṉam in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu. It seems that two shepherds
were to supply oil for the lamp.

l.ta:
kumārattiyār tevayiriammaṉ tammiṭavum umpiḷakkai ūril
tammuṭaiya taṉmamāka

tiruviḷakku[k]kum āka viṭṭa i • nattattil maṉṟāṭi tevarkkoṉmakaṉ
(makaṉ) āṭkoṇṭāṉ

cukattile irukkavum in[ta*] taṉmat[tukku*] akitampaṇṇiṉavarkaḷ
keṅkaikar[ai]yile kār[ā*]mpacuvai

koṉṟa pāvattile pokakaṭavarkaḷākavum ikkalveṭṭu
eḻutiṉamaikku koyilkaṇakku

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kṛishṇayadēva Mahārāya
Year: Śaka 1447, Sarvasittū, (sarvajit), Chittirai.

inscription.p:
Records that Śīraṅganāthar, son of a village official (maṇiyakāraṉ) of Iḍaittuṟai,
Irākkutapperumāḷ, had assigned several persons from Paḍaivīḍu as tiruviḷakkukuḍi for
supplying six kuṟuṇi of ghee every year to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār for the
merit of Namaśivāya Nāyakkar, son of Mallappa nāyakkar of Marudarasarpaḍaivīdu,
which was situated on the northern bank of the river Seyyāṟu, in Rājagambīraṉ-malai
in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of
Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.
The temple accountant, Īśura Biṟamar Rāyar Uttaṇḍi figures as signatory.

l.ta:
mel cellā(ṉ)
niṉṟa caṟuvacittu varuṣam cittirai mātam

ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭa[la*]ttu palkuṉṟa koṭṭattu melkuṉṟa
nāṭu murukama[ṅ*]kalapaṟṟu

rācakempiraṉmalaikkaṭaittapaṟṟu ce[y*]yāṟṟukku vaṭakarai maru[ta]racar
paṭaivi[ṭ]ṭu mallappanāyakkar

kumarar namacivāyanāyakkar ayyaṉukku [taṉ][ma*]māka iṭaittuṟai
maṇiyakāṟaṉ ira[kku]tapperumāḷ

makaṉ ciraṅkanātar uṭaiyār [tiru]malar uṭaiya nayiṉāṟku paṭai[viṭṭil]
iṭaituṟaiyiṟukkuṭiyai

tiruviḷakku ku[ṭiyintu] kañcaṉ ko[ṟṟaṉ] pa[ra]ma maṉṟāṭi co •
[ppaḷu] mā[ra]n makaṉ nātaṉ māraṉ u[ḷ*]ḷiṭṭār

mammaṭi[yā]ṉ nūkkaṉ [uḷ]ḷiṭṭār vempa[ṇa]ṉ makaṉ nūkkaṉ
u[ḷ*]ḷiṭṭār varuṣam oṉṟukku aḷakku
neyik[ku] inta ā[ṟu]kuṟuṇiyum varuṣamvaruṣam aḷantu cu[ka]ttileirukkavu

yātoruvar akutam [pa]ṇṇiṉavarka[ḷ] ke[ṅ]kaikaraiyile [k]ār[ā*]m
pacuvai koṉṟa pāvattile poka

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: Kṛishṇayadeva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1454. Virōdhi, Āḍi.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the assignment of the weaving tax (paṭṭaḍai-nūlāyam) by
Kāḷattīśura-Nāyakkar, at the rate of one and half-a-paṇam to be paid per loom (taṟi)
per year. The tax so levied was to be deposited in the treasury of the Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanāyiṉār temple at Dēvikkāpuram in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu
in Mēlkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.
The temple accountant, Uttaṇḍi, figures as signatory.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecura metinimicura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri cāḷuva
śrīvirappiṟatāpa kiṣṇa tevamakāirāyar piṟitiviirācciyam pa[ṇ*]ṇi
aruḷ[ā*] niṉṟa [ca]kāṟtam

ṉam tevikkāpuram tirumalaruṭaiyanā[yi]ṉār śrī
paṇṭārattukku kāḷattiyicuranāyakkar ayyaṉ taṉmam i[tu] viroti
varuṣa

u paṭṭaṭai nūlāyattukku
oṉṟaraippaṇam viḻukkāṭu koyilpaṇṭārattukku yiṟukku[m*]
cukattileyirukka kaṭaivārākavum yintataṉmattukku yātoruvar a[ku]tam
paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ kaṅkaik[ka]rai[yi]le kārāmpacu

[vai] koṉṟa t[o]ṣattile poka kaṭaivar ākavum ••••••••
• inta kalveṭṭu eḻutinamaik[ku] koyilkaṇakku uttaṇ[ṭi] •
eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadeva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1446, Chitrabānu, Tai
śu. 10, Uttiram, Saturday = irregular.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that the pontiff of Bhīksha-maṭham, Viśvēśvara Śivāchārya,
the temple officials (dānamāyēśvarar) and kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal granted one thousand
kuḻi of wet land, five hundred kuḻi of dry land and also a house, at Sōrappūṇḍi, a
dēvadāna village of the Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkapuram which was
situated to the south of Śeyyāṟu, adjacent to Rājagambīraṉ-malai in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu
in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam,
as tax free gifts (iṟaiyilikāṇiāṭchi) to Vāḍamalaiyār, one of the scholars (vidwans) of
Āṟṟuvāṉpāḍi, son of Tiruvēṅkaṭanāthar. It is also mentioned that this arrangement was
caused to be engraved on the kumudhapaḍai. Further the donors undertook the
responsibility of remitting all the taxes, like paḻavari, puduvari and other taxes, like
(levied on the lands and house).

The pontiff of Bhīksha-maṭham, Viśvēśvara Śivāchārya, the tāṉattār, Karaiēṟa-
viṭṭār, Chandranambi, kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal and several others, including the temple
accountant Uttaṇḍar figure as signatories.

l.ta:
śrīmaṉ makāmaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri
cāḷuva śrīvirappiṟatāpaśrīki[ru*]ṣṇatevamakāyirāyar piṟitivirācciyam

melcellā niṉṟa
ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟa koṭṭattu mel kuṉṟa

nāṭṭu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu yirācakempiraṉmalaikku aṭutta paṟṟu
ceyyāṟṟukku teṉpirivāṉa tevakkāpuram uṭaiyār tirumalar
uṭaiya nāyiṉār

pikṣāmaṭam vicuvecura civācāriyarum tāṉam māyecurar kaikkoḷa
mutalikaḷum āṟṟuvāṉpāṭi vittuvāṉkaḷil tiruveṅka[ṭa]nātar

makaṉ vaṭamalaiyāṟku yiṟaiili kāṇiāṭci paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi
nāṅkaḷ yivaṟku corappūṇṭiyile nañcaiyile māvaṉ

añṉūṟum oru maṉaiyum nāṅkaḷ yivaṟku iṟaiyili kāṇiāṭci

āka kumu(mu)tapaṭaiyile kalveṭṭik kuṭuttom yintamaṉai[yu*]m
nilattaiyu

eppeṟpaṭṭa cakalavakaiyu

yittai nokki vanta kāṇikkai ellām nāṅkaḷe uttiram
collikkoḷḷakkaṭavom ākavu

yimmatikārikku nañcaiyile kuḻi
āyiramum puñcaiyile kuḻi aññūṟum

puttira
pavuttira paramparai āka aṉupavittuk koḷḷak kaṭavar ākavu

yiṟaiyili kāṇiāṭci paṇṇikkuṭuttom pikṣāmaṭamvicivecura
civācāri ayyaṉ eḻuttu tāṉattār karaieṟa vi[ṭṭ]ār eḻuttu
cantira(ā)nampi eḻuttu

āḻvār tirumalainayiṉār eḻuttu māyecurar tirumalaiccivaṉ kaṅkāṇi
āṇṭār uḷḷiṭṭā[r] eḻuttu kaikkoḷamutaliyaḷ kollāviṭṭar
i ••••• ṭi nayiṉār

aṟupattu muvar nāvilaṅkātār uḷḷiṭṭa kaikkoḷamutaliyaḷ
eḻuttu yippaṭikku yintakkalveṭṭu eḻutiṉamaikku koyilkaṇakku
uttaṇṭar eḻuttu

yintataṉmattukku yātoruvar akutam paṇṇinavarkaḷ keṅkaik
karaiyile kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile pokakka[ṭ]aivarkaḷ
ākavu

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 366 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadēvarāya Year: Śaka 1444, Vikrama, Kumbha, śu. di., full moon,
Friday, uttiram = 22nd February, 1521 A.D.

inscription.p:
This records that Aṭṭavaṇai Veṅkappar, a native of Sōrappūṇḍi, had endowed (a
hamlet) Dēvakāpuram at Śeyyāṟu in Murgamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in
Palkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍalam to the god Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanayiṉār as tirunāmattukkāṇi. For this, the pontiff of the Bhīkshā-maṭha,
Īśanaśivāchāriyār, dāṉa-māyēśvarar and kaikkōḷa-mudaligaḷ gifted five hundred kuḻi of
each wet and dry lands at Sōrappūṇḍi as tax-free (iṟaiyili-kāṇippaṟṟu), two houses,
one at Sōrappūṇḍi and the other at Dēvikāpuram, again made tax-free (iṟaiyili-
kāṇiyātchi) to Veṅkappar. It is also recorded that both these lands and houses were
freed from all the old and new taxes and all other dues and these were to be borne
by the above parties. The donee was also to enjoy these lands and houses hereditarily.

Īśanaśivāchāriyār, the pontiff of Bhīkshā-maṭha at Dēvakāpuram, Karaiēraviṭṭa
Chandranambi, the tāṉattar and kaikkōḷa-mudaligaḷ and others figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟakkoṭṭattu mel kuṉṟa
nāṭu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu irācakempīraṉmalaikku aṭutta paṟṟu
ceyyāṟṟukku teṉpirivāṉa tevakkāpuram

uṭaiyār tirumalar uṭaiyanāyiṉāṟku tirunāmakkāṇiyāka
kiṟuṣṇatevamakārāyar taṉmamāka corappūṇṭi aṭṭavaṇai veṅkappar
viṭuvikkaiyil tevakkāpuram pikṣāmaṭam yicāṉaci-

vācāriyarum tāṉamāyecurar kaikkoḷamutalikaḷum aṭṭavaṇai
veṅkappaṟku kṣettiṟam iṟaiili kāṇipa-

[ṟṟu] paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi nāṅkaḷ ivaṟku corappūṇṭiyile
nañcaiyile māvaṭit tākkile aññūṟukuḻiyum puṉcaiyile eri
eturvāyile aññūṟu

kuḻiyum corappūṇṭiyile oru maṉaiyum tevakkāpuram maṇiyakāṟat
teruvile oru maṉaiyum ivaṟku nāṅkaḷ iṟaiili kāṇiyākṣiyāka
kumutappaṭaiyile kalve-

ṭṭik kuṭuttom inta maṉaiyum nilattaiyum nokki kopuravācalile
[yi]ṟakkum paḻavari putuvariyum eppeṟpaṭṭa cakalavakaiyum uḷpaṭa
[ittainokki] vanta kāri[ya]-

ṅka(ṅka)ḷ ellām nāṅkaḷe u[tta]rac collakkoḷḷak
kaṭavomākavu immatikaḷikku inta maṉai iraṇṭum nañcaiyile
aññūṟṟu kuḻiyu puṉcaiyile aññūṟṟu kuḻiyu

ili kāṇiyāṭci paṇṇikkuṭuttom tevakkāpuram pikṣāmaṭam
yicāṉa civācāriyar eḻuttu tāṉattār karaiēṟaviṭṭa cantiranampi
uḷḷiṭṭār eḻuttu

•••••••••• nayiṉār eḻuttu māyecurartirumalaiccivaṉ kaṅkāṇiccivaṉ
uḷḷiṭṭār eḻuttu kaikkoḷamutalikaḷ yicura [ka]ḷappāḷar naraciṅka
kāliṅkarāyar nā-

•••••••••• ṉār kollāviṭṭār tampirāṉ toḻar vallāṉai
veṉṟār kaṇṭiya tevar veṭanayiṉār uḷḷiṭṭa kaikkoḷamutalikaḷ
eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivarkaḷ colla eḻu-

•••••••••• uttaṇṭi eḻuttu inta taṉmattukku ahitam
paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaikkaraiyil kārāmpacuvaikkoṉṟa pāvattile
pokak kaṭavārkaḷ ākavum [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadevamahārāya Year: Śaka 1452, Sarvasiddhi
(Sarvadhāri) Kāttigai śu. 10, Saturday = Irregular.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that the temple officials (dāṉamayēśurar) conferred the
right of cultivation, uḻavukāṇiāṭchi of the land called Kāmākshirāvuttāṉi, a land owned
by the Tirumalar-uḍaiyanāyiṉār temple (tiruṉāmakkāṇi) at Dēvakkāpuram, on a certain
Ambalattāḍiyāṟ. The latter village was situated in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu, an adjacent
territory of Rajagaṁbhīraṉmalai in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-
division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is also stated that the recipient
Ambalattaḍiyār had to reclaim the land and remit 15 paṇam and twenty-kalam of paddy
annually at the temple treasury in lieu of the taxes. Further, he was also given a
house at Maṇiyakāṟa-tteṟuvu in Dēvakkāpuṟam.

The tāṉattār, Karaiēṟaviṭṭār Chandranambi and several others including Uttaṇḍi,
the temple accountant figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecurametiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri
cāḷuva śrīvirappiṟutāvaṉ kiṣṇatevamakāravirāyar

mel
cellāniṉṟa caṟuvacitti varuṣam kāttikaimātam pūṟuva

uttiramum peṟṟa caṉivārattunāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟakoṭṭattu

u aṭutta paṟṟu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu
tevastāṉam tevakkāpu

nāyiṉār tiruṉāmakkāṇiāṉa kāmākṣirāvuttāṇi tāṅkal
tāṉamayecurar kaikkoḷamutaliyaḷu[kku] te • pa • • ṟamaḷatta nayiṉār

makaṉ ampalattāṭiyāṟku uḻavukāṇiākṣi paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi nāṅkaḷ
yivaṟku kāmākṣi yirāvuttaṉ tāṅkal nañcai

puñcai uṇṭāṉatum uḻavukāṇi āka kuṭukkaiyil yiva • • •
tāṅkalile veṇṇa nilamu tiruttati

eppeṟpaṭṭa cakalau[p]ātiyum uḷpaṭa malaiyāṅkaṭṭukuttakai āka
koyilpaṇṭārattu

u meṟku maṉai oṉṟu yinta
tāṅkalum maṉaiyu

u
patiṉ añcu ••••• yirupatiṉkalanellum varuṣam varuṣam toṟum
celuttu

u akitam
paṇṇiṉava[r]kaḷ keṅkaikaraiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa pāvattile
pokakaṭaivar ākavu

yivai tāṉattār karaieṟaviṭṭa cantira[na]mpi uḷḷiṭṭār eḻuttu
yivai māyecurar tirumalaiccivaṉ kaṅkāṇi tiruppaṇi
l.note: The ward tiruppaṇi is written below the line.

l.ta:
kaikkoḷa mutaliyaḷ kaḷappāḷar kāliṅkayirāyar kollāviṭṭa •
[•••] vallāṉai [veṉṟā]r kaṇṭitevar ve[ṭa]nayiṉār [vāṇapāṭi]
•••uḷḷiṭṭār eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇayadeva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 144[4], Vikrama, Kumbha,
śu. 10. Saturday. uṇarpūsam = 16th February 1521 A.D. f.d.n. 41.

inscription.p:
Records that Ammaiyappanāyiṉār, son of Mārappar, a shepherd of Dēvakkāpuram,
a tirunāmakkāṇi (village), granted a village Ūvattūr and also its hamlet Suraipāḍi of
Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār
in Dēvakkāpuram, a village situated in the southern division of Śeyyāṟu in
Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-
maṇḍalam. It further records that he endowed these two villages as tirunāmakkāṇi to
the god for the merit of Kṛishṇadēva-mahārāya. Īśānaśivāchāriyar, a pontiff of Bhikshā-
maṭham, temple officials (tāṉamāyēśvarar), and weavers (kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal) in turn
gave these two villages as lease (kāṇippaṟṟu) to the donor. He was given the right
of inducting cultivators (kuḍiyumī-ēṟṟi) (to cultivate the land) and also the right to
enjoy the land with eight kinds of privileges (ashṭabhōgam). It is also recorded that
all the taxes such as vāśalkaḍamai, peṟkaḍamai, taṟikaḍami, śekkoṭṭu, paṭṭaḍai, nūlāyam,
kāṇikkai, kaṭṭāyam, sandai, ērimīṉvilai, kamugu, teṉṉamaram, vāḻai, karumbu, mañjaḷ iñji,
māvaḍai, maravaḍai, kōpura-vāsalil pirakkum, paḻavari, puduvari and other taxes were
waived. Instead the leasee, was to remit 100 paṇam and 120 kalam of paddy annually
at the temple treasury for the special worship of the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār
commencing from the month of Māśi from the cyclic year Vikrama onwards. Besides
this he was to enjoy this privilege hereditarily and was also given a house at Maniyakāra-
teruvu.

Īśānaśivāchāriyar, the pontiff of Bhikshā-maṭham at Dēvikkāpuram, Karaiēṟavīṭṭār
Chandranambi, the tāṉattār, Āḻvār Tirumalai Nayiṉār, and several others including the
temple accountant Uttaṇḍi figure as signatories.

l.ta:
melkuṉṟa nāṭu murukamaṅkalapaṟṟu irācakempiraṉmalaikku aṭuttapaṟṟu
ceyyāṟṟukku [teṉ]pirivāna tevakkāpuram uṭaiyār tirumalar
vuṭaiya nayiṉāṟku tirunāmakāṇi

āka tevakk[ā]puram maṉṟāṭikaḷil mārappar makaṉ ammaiyappa
nayiṉār kiruṣṇayatevamahāirāyar taṉmamāka imma

lattil ippaṟṟil
viṭṭa kiṟāmam ūvattūr kiṟāmam oṉṟu

uḷpaṭa kiṟāmam oṉṟu tirumalarvuṭaiya
nayiṉā[r] tirunāmakāṇi āka nāṉ viṭukaiyil tevakkāpuram pikṣā
ma

kaikoḷa mutalikaḷu āka i[v*]vaṉai varuṣam mārappar makaṉ ammaiyappa
nayiṉāṟku kāṇippaṟṟu āka paṇṇi kuṭuttom nāṅkaḷ kāṇi[p]paṟṟu
paṇṇi kuṭukkaiyil ivar

uḷpaṭa niti
nikṣepam akṣiṇiākāmi cittacāttiyam jalap

vācalkaṭamai peṟkaṭamai taṟikaṭamai cekkoṭṭu palataḷi paṭṭaṭai
nūlāyam kāṇikkai kaṭṭāyam cantai

ērimiṉ vilai kamuku teṉṉamaram vāḻai karumpu mañcaḷ iñci
māvaṭai maravaṭai kopuravācalil piṟakkum piḻavari

uṟama varuṣam māci mātam mutalāka varuṣam oṉṟukku
tirumalarvuṭaiya nāyiṉār mahāpūcaikku celuttu[m] 1

u kantāyapiṟāttiyile paṇamum celutti nellu aḷakka
kaṭavarākavu

mutalāka vikkiṟamavaruṣam māci mātam mutalāka
eṉṟu eṉṟai
inta kāṇippaṟṟu puttiṟapavuttiṟapāramparaiyāka cantiṟā ātitta varaiyu

u āka [ita]ṟku n[ā]ṅkaḷ viṭṭa maṉai maṇiyakāṟa
teruvile vaṭaciṟakile melai koṭiyil maṉai oṉṟum aṉupavittu
koḷḷakkaṭavarākavu

icāṉa civācāriyar eḻuttu tāṉattār
karaieṟa viṭṭār cantiranampi uḷḷiṭṭār eḻuttu āḻvārtirumalai

nayiṉār eḻuttu māyecurar tirumalaicivaṉ kaṇkāṇi civaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār
eḻuttu kaikoḷamutalikaḷ icura kaḷappāḷar naraciṅka kāliṅkarāyar
nā[vilaṅkā]tār appanāyiṉār k[o]llāviṭṭār tampirān toḻar

vallāṉai veṉṟār kaṇṭiyatevar veṭanayiṉār uḷḷiṭṭa kai
koḷamutalikaḷ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivarkaḷ colla eḻutiṉamai

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(A.R. No. 369 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1453. Virōdhi, Chittirai
śu. 10 Attam Hasta = irregular.

inscription.p:
Records that the priests (gurukkaḷ) and temple officials (tāṉamāyēsurar), mudaliyaḷ
and the ūrār of Gaṅgapuram gave the cultivating right (ūḻavukāṇiakshi) to the extent
of 750 kuḻi of wet land (called) Dēvarāyaṉ paṭṭadai at Kīḻaikkaṅgapuram in Paṅgaḷa-
nāḍu, a village which was owned by the temple (tirunāmakkāṇi) of Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvakkāpuram, to a certain Kōṉi Appanāyakkar, son of
Timmaṇaṉāyakkar, the kaṉṉaḍiyaṉāyakkapāḍi of Dēvakkāpuram. It is also stipulated
that he was free to cultivate any crop of his choice but was to remit a certain stipulated
amount of paddy every year to the temple treasury.

Īśāṉa Śivachāriyār, tāṉattār, Māyēsvarar, the temple accountant Uttaṇḍi, who had
this inscription engraved, the village accountant, Śivānadēvan, and others figure as
signatories.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecurametiṉi micura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri
cāḷuva śrīvirappiṟutāpa ki[ru*]kṣaṇaiyateva makāyirāyar piṟutivi
yirā-

mel cellā [niṉṟa*]
viroti varuṣam cittirai mātam pūṟuvapakṣamutalāka tecamiyu

tirumalar uṭaiya nāyiṉār tiruṉāmakkāṇi āṉa paṅkaḷa
ṉāṭṭil

kiḻaikkaṅka[puram] eri e[tu]vāyi • • ṉāyakkaṉtāṅkal kiḻ tevarāyaṉ
paṭṭaṭai nañcaiku

ūravarum meṟpaṭi ūṟ kaṉṉaṭiyaṉāyakkapāṭi timmaṇaṉāyakkar
makaṉ koṉi appaṉāyakkaṟku uḻavukāṇiākṣi paṇṇikkuṭuttom

yinta nilam tām veṇṭum payirceytu koṇṭum malaiyāṅkāṭṭu
[ku]ttakai āka koyilpaṇṭārattu

celut[ti*] tām veṇṭum payiṟceytu koṇṭu cukattileyirukka
kaṭaivar ākavu

civāc[ā*]riyar eḻu[t*]tu tāṉattār eḻuttu m[ā]yecu[ra]r
eḻu[t*]tu kaḷappāḷar kāliṅkayirāyar vallāṉai veṉṟār uḷḷi[ṭṭ]ār
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇayadēva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1450, ŚArvadhāri, Āṉi,
śu. Uttiram. Monday = irregular.
p.note:
The Annual Report fixed the date on Monday, 22nd June 1528 A.D. It is assumed śu. 5. Even
in this date the star is not tallied.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the supply of one padakku of ghee every year by (the
measure) śūlakkāl for a lamp to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvakkapuram,
a dēvasthānam in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu, by the two shepherds (residing at) Kailāyam,
Nūrkaṇaṉ and Sevvaṇṇaṉ. The temple accountant Uttaṇḍi, who had this epigraph
engraved, tāṉattār, māyēsvarar and Kaḷappāḷar Kāliṅgarayar, the Kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal
figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecurametiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri
cāḷuvakakṣaṇaiya

teva makāyirāyar piṟutiviyirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷā
niṉṟa cakāttam

cāṟuvatārivaruṣattu pūṟuvapakṣattu āṉimātamum uttiramum peṟṟa
comavārattu nāḷ murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu tevastāṉam tevakkāpu

kum āka namacivāyanāyakkar ayyaṉ taṉ[ma]m āka viṭṭa kayilāyattil
yiru

u maṉṟāṭikaḷil kāṭṭuayyappu makaṉ ṉūrkkaṇṉum
cevvaṇ[ṇa*]ṉ uḷḷiṭṭāṟkum cāta(ta)kaṭamai kāṇi-

u aṉṟā[ṭu] cūlakkālil neyi 0 yinta neyipatakkum
varuṣamtoṟu aḷantu cukattileyirukka kaṭavar ākaṣa

akutam paṇṇinavarkaḷ keṅkaikkaraiyile kār[ā*]mpacuvaik koṉṟa
pāvattile

ivai tāṉattār eḻuttu māyecura[r*] eḻuttu kaikkoḷamutaliyaḷ
kaḷappāḷa[r] kāliṅkarāyar eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 371 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: Kṛishṇadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1443; Vikrama, Pañchami Monday. 1521 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that (a lady) Āradēvarāchchi-āyi-ammaṉ, daughter of Surāchchiy-āyi-
ammaṉ of Sīmarudarasar paḍaivīdu, got two kāṇi of ghee supplied annually on the
occasion of Kārttigai (for burning lamps) to Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvakkapuram
in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu by two kuḍis (shepherds) Siṅgakkōṉ Iḷaiyaperumāḷ and
Māṉakkōṉ Kariyāṉ chēraṉ who were residents of Soṟṟukkuṉṟi, an umbaḷikkai village
of the donor.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍi wrote this epigraph (conveying this charity).

l.ta: ṉam tevakāpuram tirumalaruṭaiya nāyiṉāṟku ci marutara-
ammaṉ tammiṭa umpaḷakkai ūril tammuṭa taṉmamāka tiruviḷakku
ceraṉ uḷḷiṭṭār varuṣattukku aḷakku neyi ta āka kuṭi

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 372 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇadeva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1431, Śukla, Makara, śu. 10,
Sunday, Tiruvōṇam = 23 September 1509 A.D. However the above set
of dates tally with Kaṉṉi not with Makara.

inscription.p:
Records that Tirumalai-nāyakkar, son of Ettappa-nāyakkar, Gurukkaḷ, (priests),
trustees (tāṉattār) devotees (māyēśvarars) and weaving merchants (kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal)
granted a piece of land (called) Siṅgayerāvuttar-tāṅgal located in the hamlet of
Kayilāyam, which was owned by the temple (Śūpaṇḍārakkāṇi) of Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkapuram, a dēvasthānam in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu
(situated) next to Rājagambhīranmalai, as kāṇiyākshi to Tiruppaṇi Vasavaṇa-uḍaiyar
for the merit of the ruler Kṛishṇadeva Mahārāya. It further records that the donee
was free to cultivate the land and grow crops of his choice. Besides this he was also
to receive four kalam of paddy from the temple treasury.

It seems that the donee was to measure out certain amount of produce at the
temple treasury to meet the expenditure of the temple worship.

l.ta:
ṉmakāmaṇṭalecurametiṉi micura kaṇṭa
kaṭṭāri cāḷuva śrīvirappiṟutāpa kikṣaṇaitevamakāyirāyar
piṟutiviyirācciyam paṇṇi a[ru]ḷāniṉṟa cakāṟtam
lavaruṣattu makaranāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu tecamiyum nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyu

tiruv[o*]ṇattunāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭala[t*]tu palkuṉṟa
koṭṭattu melkuṉṟanāṭu yirācakempiraṉmalai

aṭutta paṟṟu murukamaṅkalappaṟṟu tēvastāṉu tevakkā[ yum] tirumalar
uṭaiya nāyiṉār śrīpaṇṭārakkāṇi āṉa kayilāyattil piṟivu

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 373 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, of the second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achyutadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1452 Vikṛiti, Makara
śu. di. 13, Monday. Pūśam = January 30. 1531 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that the (pontiff) Īśānaśivachāriyār of Bhīkshā-maṭha, who was also an
official of the treasury of the Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvakkapuram, the
tāṉattār, the māyēśvarars, and the kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal gave the right of cultivation
(kāṇiāṭchchi) of the village Suvarappūṇḍi, a tirunāmakkāṇi village of the Tirumalar-
uḍaiyār temple inorder to conduct offerings, worship, the maintenance of the sacred
lamps, and the performance of temple service (tiruppaṇi) in the Sadāśivamūrtti temple,
at Vaḍa-kailāyam, a tirunāmakkāṇi village of the Tirumalar temple, which was built
by Tirumalai-Nāyakkar, son of Sadāśiva-nāyakkar, who resided at Marudarasarpaḍaivīḍu.
It is further mentioned that Tirumalai-Nāyakkar remitted a sum of three hundred
paṇam, as price-money, inorder to reclaim the fallow lands and excavate a big tank
to make the lands at the ceded village, fit for cultivation.

It further states that (kāṇi holders) were free to cultivate any crop such as
sugarcane, plantain, turmeric, and others and were free to induct cultivators. Taxes
such as paḻavari, puduvari, kāṇikkai, kaṭṭāyam, mugampāṟvai and other taxes were also
remitted. Īśāna-sivachāriyār, the pontiff and the tāṉattār, Tirumalainambi and others
including the temple accountant Uttaṇḍi figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecurar metiṉi micura kaṇṭakaṭṭāri cāḷuvaṉ
ariyar[ā]ya vipāṭaṉ pāṣaikku tappuvar[ā]yar kaṇṭaṉ muvar[ā]yar

ūṟku teṟku muttappaṭaiyar tāṅkal varampaḻa karampu aṉāti moṟai

āyi irukkaiyil puticākat tāṅkalum veṭṭi tāṅkal kiḻ
kaḻaṉiyu

makaṉār tirumalai nāyakkar cuvarappūṇṭi periya eri veṭṭa kuṭutta ø

ivai kaḷappāḷar kāliṅkarāyar kollāviṭṭa vāṇ[ṭa]rayar
uḷḷiṭṭa kaikkoḷamutaliyaḷ eḻuttu yippaṭikku yintakalveṭṭu
eḻutiṉamaikku koyilkaṇakku uttaṇṭar eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achyutayyadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1454, Vikṛiti,
Paṅguni śu. 11. Monday = irregular.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the dedication of certain shepherds, belonging to Śeṟṟuppaṭṭu
as tiruviḷakkukuḍi of the Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār temple at Dēvākkapuram, a
dēvasthānam in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam, (a sub-
division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Koṇḍama-nāyakkar the father-in-law of
Mallayapa-nāyakkar, a aḍaippattu (official). The shepherds were to measure out two
kalam, one tūṇi and one padakku of ghee for burning lamps for the merit of the reigning
king Achyutadēva Mahārāya. It is also mentioned that he dedicated their services after
obtaining the consent of Kāḷattīśuranāyakkar, who was governing Paḍaivīḍu-sīrmai.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍi, son of Appar, wrote this deed on stone.

l.ta:
śrīvirappiṟatāpa naraca[r] nāyakkar uṭaiyar kumārar acci(ā)tatevamakā
irāyar

nāyakkaṟku paṭaiviṭṭuccirmai cellukaiyil ivar kaiyil aṭaippattu
mallayapa-

nāyakkar māmaṉār koṇṭamanāyakkar vāṅki viṭṭa ce[ṟ]ṟupaṭṭu
maṉṟāṭi vella-

cceri nallam piṉṉāṇṭi uḷḷiṭṭārum vempilmāru uḷḷiṭṭārum
kaṟuttaccevvaṉum polum naṉṉāṇṭi

kārakap piṉṉāṇṭi cevvaṉum kūṉaṉcevvaṉēṟaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārum
poṟuceṭṭi

makaṉpettaṉum cantu [man] •••• makaṉa • peṟaṉum tarukkaṉ[pe]ttaṉum
tiritta eṟu makaṉ

pettaṉ vempa[ci]ṇa makama maṇamum • • makaṉ [opaci]ntaṇa nāyakkar
makaṉ cintaṇaṉ

kku aḷakku ita
kaṇṇaṉūr maṉṟāṭi vaiyaṇaṉ koṭun poṉṉum [pe]

paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaikaraiyile kārāmpacuvaik koṉṟa
pāvattile pokak

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 375 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achuyutadēva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1452, Vikruti, siṁha,
śu. 14, Sunday, Tiruvōṇam = 7th August 1530 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that the 1. pontiff of Bhīkshā-maṭham Īśuraṉ Śivāchāriyār
who was responsible for the treasury of Tirumalar-uḍaiyanāyiṉār temple at
Dēvakkāpuram, a dēvadānam in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu, 2. tāṉattār 3. Māyēsvarar and
4. kaikkōḷa-mudaḷigal had conferred the lease or the right of cultivation
(uḻavukāṇiāṭchi) of some fallow lands at Piramaṇampākkam on Viṟamāyēsvarar
Mallikāṟchiṉa Ayyaṉ maṭha at Sembiyamaṅgalam in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu (a sub-division)
of Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam. The inscription also states that the leasee was to reclaim the land
and was free to cultivate the crops of his choice. The boundaries of the land allotted
were also given. Taxes such as puñjaikaḍamai, vāśalkaḍamai, taṟikkaḍamai,
peṟkaḍamai, sekkōṭṭu, talaiyārikkam, paṭṭaḍai, nūlāyam, iḍaittīṟai, veṭṭivari, etc., were
waived, instead he was to remit a certain amount of taxes every year to the temple
treasury, which in turn was used to conduct the offerings and worship of the god,
Tirumalar-uḍaiyanāyiṉār (when he was pleased to appear) at Vasantaṉ-maṇḍapa. The
mode of levying these taxes every year was given as follows:- First year 5 paṇam,
5 kalam of paddy, Second year (kara) 10 paṇam, 10 kalam of paddy, Third year
(Nandana) 15 paṇam, 15 kalam of paddy. Fourth year (Viśaya) 20 paṇam, 20 kalam
of paddy. Fifth year (Jaya) 30 paṇam, 30 kalam of paddy, an amount which was
fixed as permanent tax.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍar wrote this deed on stone.

l.ta:
u
tappuvarāyarkaṇṭaṉ muvarāyarkaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu
koṇṭanāṭu kuṭātāṉ emmaṇṭalamum tiṟaikoṇṭa yintu kāṇi

allāḷamaṅkalattil ellaittaṟuvāy kaṭaikkūṭṭu nayiṉām

u uṭpaṭa nilam uṇṭāṉatu tām uḻavukāṇi āka

ēṟṟikkoṇṭu

payiṟ ceyyumiṭattu

kamuku teṉṉamaram nañcai

vācalkaṭamai taṟikkaṭamai peṟkaṭamai cekkoṭṭu

kopuravācalil piṟa
um paḻavari putuvari nallerutu naṟpacu koṇikai

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 376 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: Achyutadēva Mahārāya Year: Sāṟvani, Māśi 14.

inscription.p:
Records that Mallappa-Nāyakkar and Sēvvappa-Nāyakkar appointed certain
Bhagavāṉ Kōvaraṉ, son of Viḷattarkōṉ, a shepherd of Kaṇiyaṉūr, as shepherd
(tiruviḷakkukuḍi) to supply three kuṟuṇi of ghee by the śūlakkāl measure (for burning
lamps) in the temple of the god Tirumalar-uḍaiya tambiṟāṉār of Dēvakkāpuram, on
the occasion of Kārttigai, in order to secure merit of the king Achyutadēva Mahārāya.

l.ta:
ayyaṉum cevvappanāyakkar ayyaṉum tevakkāpuram tirumalar uṭaiya
tampirāṉāṟku tiruviḷakkukuṭi āka kalavai

ppaṟṟu iṭattuṟaikku vācapaṭi cantiraṉukku iṟuttiṟkuṭi kaṇiyaṉūr
maṉṟāṭi viḷattakkoṉ makaṉ pakavāṉ

ko[vara]ṉ tiruviḷakkum āka niṟceyittu aḷakkum neyi • inta
neyi mukkuṟuṇiyum cūlakkālile tirukkoyilukku

tirukkāttikaikku aḷantu (cu) cukattile [ku]ṭiirukka kaṭaivar ākavum
inta taṉmam mallappanāyakkar ayyaṉ

cevvappaayyaṉ taṉmaāka naṭakka[ka*]ṭaivar ākavum inta taṉma[t*]tukku
iyātoruvar akutam(pa) paṇṇiṉa (te) uṇṭāṉāl

keṅkai[k]karaiyile kārāmpacuvaik ko[ṉ]ṟa pāvattile
pokakkaṭaivar ākavum §

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Vīraprātapa Achyutadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1452, Vikruti, siṁha, śu 11
Friday, Anusham = irregular.

inscription.p:
It records that the ūravar of Māmaṇampākkam in Maṇḍakuḷa-nāḍu in
Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, Amarakōṉār,
Kuḷattuḻār Chittiramēḻipparaiyar, and Mūvarāya-nāyakkar granted six hundred kuḻi of
fallow land at Allāl-maṅgalam, freed from taxes, (as dēvadānam-sarvamānyam) to the
god to conduct worship at Vasanthaṉ-maṇḍapam to the god in the temple of Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvakkāpuram, which was situated to the south of Seyyāṟu. It is
stated that this charity was made for the merit of Tirumalai-Nāyakkar, Kāḷattisura-
Nāyakkar, Mīṉāmmā Śivammaṉ, and Immaḍi Tirumalai-Nāyakkar. The temple was
to reclaim the land and cultivate any crops. The members of the ūr, Amarakōṉār,
and Kuḷattūḻār Chitramēḻiparaiyiar, the ūrkaṇakku, Koṇḍaiyyaṉ and others figure as
signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 378 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: Sadāśivarāya Year: Śaka 1[4]71 saumya; 1549 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Begins with the praśasti of the king Sadāśivarāya, the king has sāḷuva
title. Records the supply of ghee, for the merit of Tirumalar Nāyakkar, the son . .
••••• of Nāyakkar •••• Paḍaivīḍu.

The temple accountant Vīrappaṉ ••••••ḍattūṟai Aḷgar wrote the epigraph which
contains the endowment.

l.ta: ṉmakāmaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri cāḷuva
catāciva irāyar piṟutivirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa cakāṟ[ta*]m

virappaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 379 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Records the shepherds (maṇrāḍis) namely Saṅkarakkōṉ, Periyavīran and others
of (Dēvikkāpuram) in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam
(a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, agreed to measure out one kuruṇi of
ghee, in lieu of [the tax] Sādakkaḍamal, at the temple treasury every year. Probably
they supplied ghee (for the ritual services) instituted by Tirumalaināthar Piḷḷai.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍar figures as the signatory of this record.

l.ta:
keṅkaik karaiyile kār[ā]mpacuvaik koṉṟa pāvattile pokak
kaṭaivarkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Vīraprātapa A[chyu]tayyadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1455, Hēviḷambi
(wrong), Mārgaḻi, śu•••Puṇarpūsam, Monday.

inscription.p:
Records that two shepherds Eṭṭukkōṉ and Āṉaikkōṉ, who had been supplying
ghee, to ••••••Sembiyam Maṅgalam Iḍaittuṟaippaḍaivīḍu, had to measure out one
tūṇi and one padakku of ghee to the temple in order to burn a lamp daily on the
mountain for the (god) Tirumalar-uḍaiya tambirāṉār of Dēvakkapuram, for the merit
of Piḻaipoṟuttār Tirumalaināthar. It is also stated that the accountant of the temple
and ūr, Uttaṇḍi engraved the inscription.

l.ta:
[śrī*]maṉmakāmaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇ[ṭa*]
kaṭṭāri cāḷuva śrīvirappiṟatā
tirumalaruṭaiya tampirā[ṉā*]ṟku tirumalai mele tiṉamum oru viḷa

tirumalainātarpiḷḷaikku puṇṇiya[m*] āka periyamutaliyār piḷḷai[ kku]
••••• cempiyam

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
The inscription records that Nayiṉappa Nāyakkar, an agent of the official
(aḍaippa) Mallappa Nāyakkar made a gift of two lamps for burning during day time
(pakal-viḷakku) to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanāyiṉār at Dēvakkāpuram, for the merit
of his master, by arranging to supply a kalam of ghee every year by the shepherds
including Timmaṇṇaṉ Teṉṉērkoṇḍāṉ. It is stated that they undertook the responsibility
of measuring the ghee for celebrating the occasion of Tirukkārttigai.
The temple accountant Vīrappaṉ had the inscription engraved.

ayyaṉ kāriyattukkukkaṭaivar nayiṉappaṉākkar malla-

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 382 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achyutayyadēva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1458, Manmatha, Mīna, śu. di. 10, Friday,
Tiruvōṇam = March, 1536 A.D. However, the cyclic year was, Durmukha, and the tithi being ba. 10.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Contains the panegyric portion and also some land grant. Certain goddess
shrine (Nāchchiyār kōyil) is mentioned. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 383 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. Records the gift twelve poṉ for conducting worship to the god Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanāyiṉār of Dēvikkāpuram on the day of Uttirat-tirunāḷ by Nayiṉappa Nāyakkar,
an official of Aḍaippattu Mallappa Nāyakkar, for the merit of Śēvappa Nāyakkar.
The temple accountant and Uttaṇḍa piḷḷai, and Dharmarāsan piḷḷai and others figure
as signatories.

l.ta:
manmakāmaṇṭalecuraṉ metiṉi micura ••••••• ṭaṉ
pāṣaikku tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achuyutayyadeva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1456 Jaya, Kaṉṉi, śu.
di. 10 Monday = Sept 17, 1534 A.D. but the Week day was Thursday.

inscription.p:
Records that the taxes collected from the residents of Sembiyamaṅgalam and
Kuṟumbattuṟai, Viraṇa Kavundaṉ, Timmaṇa Kavundaṉ, Puchchiṟ Vīrappaṉ, Śekkumattāli
Pugaikuriya . . ., Periyanāthaṉ, Periyavīrappaṉ, Paṭṭappaṉ and Mallappaṉ who paid taxes
such as vaḍugaṟai, mugampārvai and other taxes amounting to 71(1/2) paṇam were
to be remitted to the temple treasury by Tirumalaināthar Piḷḷai, the younger brother
of Piḻaipōṟuttar Piḷḷai in order to conduct a festival on the occasion of Pavitturai-tirumāḷ
in the month of Āḍi (in the above temple) to the god Tirumalaruḍaiya Tambirāṉār
of Dēvakkāpuram for the merit of Achuyutayyadēva-Mahārāya.

l.ta:
ṉ makāmaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri cāḷuva
śrī vi[ṟa]piṟatāya

ṉināyaṟṟu tecamiyum peṟṟa comavārattu nāḷ tevakkāpuram
tirumalaruṭaiya tampirāṉāṟku āṭi mātam pavatturai

tirunāḷ accutaiyatevamakāirāyaṟku taṉmam āka piḻai
poruttarpiḷḷai tampiyār tirumalainātar piḷḷai

karaiyile kārāmpacuvai koṉṟa pāvattile pokakkaṭaivarkaḷ
ākavu

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 385 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged at the end and letters are not neatly engraved. Seems to assign some
(shepherds) Ulakītti, son of Maṇampoṉṉikōṉ of Koṉṉandaparambil, Tirumalai, and
Paramaṉ as tiruviḷakkukuḍi to supply six (kuṟuṇi ?) of ghee by the measure sūlakkāl
annually to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvakkapuram, for the merit of
the king.

l.ta:
accu[taiya]teva makāirāyar piṟutiviirā[c*]ciyam pa[ṇ*]ṇi
aruḷāniṉṟa cakāṟ[ta*]m

l ••••• ṟa ceya[ṅk]oṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟa koṭṭattu

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 386 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b: King: Achytadēva mahārāya Year: Śaka 1454, Nandana, Āvaṇi 25.

inscription.p:
Records that Koṇḍama Nāyakkar of Śeṟṟuppaṭṭu granted land at Vaṇṇāntāṅgal
in Kaṇṇaṉūr as uḻavukāṇi to the god, uḍaiyār Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār at
Dēvakkāpuram, a dēvasthāṉam in Palkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, for
the merit of the king, Achchudadēva mahārāya. Further he is also said to have made
arrangements for the remittance of ten paṇam every year for conducting the waking
up ceremony (tiruppaḷḷi-eḻuchchi) of the above god.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍar figures as signatory.

l.ta:
ṉmakāmaṇṭalecura metiṉi micura kaṇṭa kaṭṭāri cāḷuva
accutatevamakārāyar piṟutivirācciyam paṇṇi aruḷā-

niṉṟa cakāṟtam 1454- mel cellāniṉṟa naṉtaṉavaruṣam
āvaṇimātam 25 ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalam palkuṉṟa nāṭu

accutatevamakārāyaru[k*]ku tanmamāka ceṟṟuppaṭṭu koṇṭamaṉāyakkar-

viṭṭa kaṇṇaṉūr vaṇṇāntāṅkal vacivarāci uḻavukāṇiyāka
paṇṇikkoṇṭu tirumalaruṭaiya nayi[ṉā*]rukku tiruppa[ḷ*]ḷi eḻicci

yinta paṇam pattum
varuṣam varuṣam vettu cukattileyirukka kaṭavarākavum

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmbā temple.

p.b:
King: Sadāśivadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1470 A.D. Saumya
(wrong); Rishabha śu. full moon, Monday, Tiruvōṇam = irregular.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a gift of land, owned by the temple (tirunāmakkāṇi)
of Nayinār Tirumalar uḍaiya Nayinār in Murugamaṅgalapaṟṟu in Palkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, to a certain ••••• vāruṇa [Siva] Nāyakkar, as kāṇiyāṭchi.
Vīrappaṉ, the temple accountant, figures as signatory of the record.

l.ta:
ṉmakāmaṇṭalecuraṉ catācivateva makārāyar
piṟutivi yirācciyam paṇṇi

mel cellā niṉṟa cavumiyavaruṣattu
iṣapanāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu

pavurṇamiyum comavāramum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattunāḷ
ceyamkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟakkoṭṭa[t*]tu

maṅkalappaṟṟu nayiṉār tirumalaruṭaiya nayiṉār tirunāmakāṇiyāṉa
pariyantāṅkal taṟmaracaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 388 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, second prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Veṅkaṭadēva mahārāya Year: Śaka 1512, sukla, Tai 21.
Monday, pūśa = irregular.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains the epithets of the ruling king, Veṅkaṭadēva mahārāya,
son of Tirumalaidēva mahārāya, such as Rāyasuradhāraṇaṉ, Irāśidhirāsan,
Rāsaparamēśvaraṉ, Rāśarishabharāśa Dēvēndrarāśasūḍāmaṇi, Navakōṭi Nārāyaṇaṉ,
Ēkāṅgavīrāṉ and others.

It records the gift of land (which was situated) to the west of the temple at
Dēvakkāpuram by Nayinnappanāyakkar, son of Kṛishṇappanāyakkar, to the temple
at Dēvakkāpuram, for the merit of Koṇḍammāḷ, wife of Daḷavarāya
Timmappanāyakkaraiyyaṉ and also for the sake of Sidisiraṉ and Periyanāyakkiammai.
It further records that the land being a fallow, the donor reclaimed it, excavated tanks
and dug wells and made it cultivable and donated it. The temple accountant Nallatambi
figures as signatory.

l.ta:
kka mokām
tavittāṉ yiḻamtiṟaikoṇṭa yintu rāyacurataraṇaṉ

yirācātirācaṉ [rā]ca[pa]ramecuraṉ rācariṣapa rāca kuñcara yi[rā*]ca
maṉmata yirācarum yirācaṉārāyaṇaṉ (te) teventira rācacūṭāmaṇi

viraciṅkanāṉa pūṟuvatakṣaṇa paccimauttara cap(pi)tacamuttirātipati
navakoṭinārāyaṇaṉ ēkāṅkaviraṉ tiṉavātacūraṉ viru-

tu nārapālaṉ keṭimaṉṉiyar curataraṇan śrīmaṉ makāmaṇṭalecura
tirumalaitevamakā[rāyar] kumāra[r] veṅkaṭa
teva maka(ā)rāyar piṟuti[vi*]rācciyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa cakāṟtam

palkuṉṟa koṭṭattu melkuṉṟaṉāṭu murukama[ṅka*]lapaṟṟu rācakempira
tuṟkattu

eriyum veṭṭiviccu nilamtiruttiviccu kiṇaṟukaḷum uṇṭākki
taḷavarāya timmappaṉāyakkarayyaṉ peṇcāti koṇṭa-

l.note: These two letter are engraved below.

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(A.R. No. 389 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Naṭarāja shrine Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Kṛishṇayadēva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1436, Śrīmukha,
Karkaṭka śu. 10, Friday, uttiram = May 13, 1513 A.D.
but the month was vrishabha not Karkaṭka. And Śaka year
was 1435 and not 1436.

inscription.p:
It records that 1. Visvēśura Āchchāriyar, the pontiff of Bhikshā-maṭham, who was
responsible for the treasury of the temple at Dēvakkāpuram, a dēvasthānam of
Rājagambhīranmalai in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu, 2. the tāṉattār 3. Kaḷappāḷar of
Sembiyamaṅgalam, 4. Adiyamāṉār 5. and the ūravar, conferred the right of cultivation
(uḻavukāṇiāṭchchi) of the hamlet Sembiyamaṅgalam, a village owned by the Tirumalar-
uḍaiyanāyiṉār temple (tirunāmakkāṇi), on Namaśivāya Nāyakkar, son of Mallappa
Nāyakkar, the Kaṉṉaḍiyanāyakkarpāḍigaḷ, who resided at Marudarasarpaḍaivīḍu.

He was to reclaim the land and to induct the cultivators (kuḍiyaḍmēṟṟi) (to
cultivate the land). It is also recorded that taxes, kāṇikkai, kaṭṭāyam, poṉvaṟgam,
nelvaṟgam, māvaḍai, maravaḍai, vāḻai, karumbu, mañjaḷ, iñji, kamugu, teṉṉamaram, etc.,
were waived. Instead he was to remit 10 paṇam and 10 kalam of paddy annually
commencing from the month of Āḍi to Āṉi from the year, Śrīmukham. Next year
he was to pay 20 paṇam and 20 kalam of paddy, next year, (third year) 30 paṇam
and 30 kalam of paddy and in the next year, ie., fourth year (Īśura-var-usham) fifty
panam and fifty kalam of paddy at the temple treasury. He was also given a house.

The pontiff of Bhikshā-maṭham, Visuvēsuraśivāchāriyār, tāṉattār, Tirumalaināyakka
nambi, and others including the temple accountant Īsurabiṟumādirāyar Eṭṭiyār figure
as signatories.

l.ta:
ṣaṇaiya teva makārāyar piṟuti[vi*]rācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷā niṉṟa
cakāṟtam

nāyaṟṟu pūṟuvapakṣattu cukkiṟavāramum tecamiyum peṟṟa uttirattu
nāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟakoṭṭattu melkuṉṟa
nāṭu murukamaṅkalapaṟṟu i[rāca]kempiraṉmalaikku aṭaitta paṟṟu

ṉam tevakkāpuram nayiṉār tirumalaruṭaiya nayinār śrī
paṇṭārattukku kaṭavarāṉa piṣṣā

maṭam vicuvecuraācāriyarum
tāṉattārum ivaṉaivarum cempiyamaṅkalam kaḷappāḷarum atiyamāṉārum
maṟṟum uḷḷiṭṭa ūravarum mā-

mutal āṉimātam varaikku varuṣamoṉṟukku tirumalar uṭaiya nayiṉār

iruppukku kāṇiāṭcci āka

vaḷappār caṉṉatitteruvil teṉ
ciṟakil kaḷappāḷar tam piṉviṭṭukku kiḻakkum vā[y]kku meṟkum
uḷpaṭṭa maṉaiyum ivaṟku kāṇiāṭcci āka caṉtirātittavaraikkum
paṇṇi kuṭuttamaikku

pikṣāmaṭam vicuvecuracivaāc[ā*]riyar eḻuttu tāṉattār tirumalai
nāyakkanampi caṉtiranampi karaiye[ṟa] viṭṭār eḻuttu āḻvār
tirumalainayiṉār eḻuttu tirumalaiccivaṉ eḻuttu kaikoḷamutaliyaḷ
kaḷappāḷar

kāliṅkarāyar tampirāṉ toḻarvāṇṭār caṉtira nāyaṉār aṟupattu
muvar [ko]llāviṭṭār eḻuttu kaṇkāṇi āṇṭār malaipperumāḷ
eḻuttu cempiyamaṅkalam ūravar kaḷappāḷar atiyamāṉār eḻuttu

ūrkaṇakku aṉutāriyār eḻuttu ivarkaḷ coṟpaṭikku inta
kalveṭṭu eḻutiṉamaikku koyilkaṇakku yicurapiṟumātirāyar eṭṭiyār
eḻuttu § namaccivāya [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Naṭaraja shrine, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: ⎯⎯ Year: Śaka 1437, Dunmati, Jyeshṭa śu.
12, Monday Mṛigasīrisha 1515 A.D. But the other
deatails are irregular.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Purport not clear. It seems to record gift of land at Kailāsam, a hamlet,
to a person, Kāśināthadeivaruḷ Perumāḷ. Also refers to a certain Viśvēśvara Śivāchāryār,
the manager (kōyil-kēḻvi) of the temple but the context is not clear. It also seems to
register gift to the temple for conducting some rituals.

edition.l:
tu[ nmati] samvaṟtu sarattu ce[ṣama] cutta tu[vā]daśiyum intu vāramum
peṟṟa mira[ga]śir nakṣatrattu nāḷ koyilkeḻvi śē-

l.ta: yum maṉai oṉṟu uṭpaṭa aṭṭayile aṉu-

edition.p: There is one more piece which runs as follows:—

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the kitchen, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Sadāśivadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1468, Parābhava,
Vṛischika, Tiruvōṇam, Friday = 26 November 1546 A.D.

inscription.p:
It records that Sūrappanāyakkar got one padakku of oil per year supplied by the
shepherd Poṟāṅkōṉṉāchchi and others at Pūṇḍi to the god, Tirumalar-uḍaiya-tambirāṉār,
for the merit of Kṛishṇappanāyakkar.

l.ta: varuṣam oṉṟukku aḷakku neyi inta neyipatakku

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west wall, kitchen, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achuyutadēva-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1453, Vikruti, śu. di
Tiruvōṇam Wednesday = 20th April 1530, but the tithi was bahuḷa.

inscription.p:
Fragmentary. Contains only the names of the territorial divisions Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu
in Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. Other details are lost.

l.ta: pūṟuvapakṣattu tiruv[o*]ṇamum peṟṟa putavārat[tu] nāḷ ceyaṅko-

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
It is in Grantha characters of the 13-14th century A.D. It is in verse. It eulogises
Visvēśvarāchārya, who is stated to have been the foremost devotee of Śrīgirīśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 394 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Achyutadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 145[1], Virōdhi,
Paṅguṉi, 18.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record a gift of eighty (paṇam ?) by Mallappanāyakkar, an
official (aḍaippattu) of the ruler Achchutayadēva Mahārāya for conducting offerings
and worship to the god, Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār at Dēvukāpuram.
The temple accountant Uttaṇḍi figures as signatory.

edition.l: ṭṭu eḻutiṉamai[ kku]

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Immaḍi Narasiṁha-mahārāya. Year: Śaka 1426, Raktākshi,
Kaṉṉi, ba. 1, Wednesday, Aśvati = 25th September 1504 A.D.

inscription.p:
It records that Tirumalai Nāyakkar, an agent (kāriyattukku-kaḍavār) of
Narasanāyakkauḍaiyār, granted 1200 kuḻi of wet land at Sembiyamaṅgalam, a
tirunāmakkāṇi village of the temple at Dēvikāpuram, one kuṟuṇi of rice daily, and
two paṇam per month and also two houses at Dēvakkāpuram to a priest
Paḻakiṉaipperumāḷ, son of Tirukuraku-uḍaiyār, a priest of the Mahāgaṇapati temple
at Paḍaivēḍu, for performing worship of the god, Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār and goddesses
Periyanāchchiyār at Dēvakkāpuram in Murgamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in
Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍalam. It is also recorded that
the above donee was entitled to enjoy these properties perpetually and the taxes on
land, āḷamañji, nīrkūli and others were remitted infavour of the priest. Further the
record also mentions that Ekkappanāyakkar, father of (the donor), on earlier days,
granted land and conducted worship.

l.ta:
vakkāpuram tirumalar uṭaiyār periya nācciyār
pūcaiyum

ṉ paḻakiṉaipperumāḷukku muṉnāḷ appaṉ
ekkappaṉāyakkar pūcai

varu[kaiyā]l iṉṉāyaṉār tirunāmakkāṇiyāṉa cempiyamaṅkalattil
erikiḻ nañcai nilattile āyirattu irunūṟu kuḻiyum nittiyam kuṟuṇi
ariciyum mātam iraṇṭu

paṇamum tevakkapurattile iraṇṭu maṉaiyum peṟṟu tirumalariuṭaiya
nayiṉāraiyum periyanācciyāraiyum pūcaipaṇṇi
vikka kaṭavarākavum iṉtanilattai nokki āḷamañci niṟku
yil kār[ā]mpacuvai koṉṟa pāpattile pokakaṭavar ākavum

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura of the same temple, right of entrance.

p.b:
King: Immaḍi Narasiṁha-mahārāya. Year: Śaka 1426, Raktākshi,
Kumbha ba. di. 14. Sunday, Tiruvōṇam, Śivarātri = 2nd February 1505 A.D.

inscription.p: Clumsily engraved as the stone was not dressed before the inscription was engraved.

It records that on the auspicious occasion of Śivarāttiri, Samarapuṅga-Dīkshitaṉ,
son of Kiḍāmbi Ananda Bhaṭṭaṉ of Atrēya-gōtra and Āpastaṁba-sūtrā donated,
Maṇalpākkam alias Sōmāsipuram, a village situated on the south bank of (the river)
Śeyyāṟu in Tachchūr-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam in Murgamaṅgalappaṟṟu, to the goddess
Periyanāchchiyār of Dēvikipuram for (supplying) oblations. It is also stated that this
village was given to the donor by Tirumalai-Nāyakkar in the earlier days for his
performance of agnihōtrā (keeping the sacred fire).

The inscription concludes with the usual imprecatory versed found in the
Vijayanagar records.

edition.l:
svastiśrī manmahāmaṇḍalēśvara mēdanimisara gaṇḍakaṭhāri sāḷuva
(sāḷuva) narasiṁharāyar ( rāyar) kumārar immaṭi narasiṁharāya
mahārāyar prathivirājyam paṇṇi aruḷā niṉṟa cakābdam 1426l

koṭṭattut taccūrnāṭṭu ceyyāṟṟukkut t[e]ṉkaraiy maṇal
pākkamā-

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: ⎯⎯ Year: Kali 4990, Śaka 1811, Virōdhi,
1889 A.D., November 27.

inscription.p:
It records that Abhinavapūrṇapriya Śrīnivāsa Rao sahēb, Jaghirdār of Āraṇi, piously
performed ashṭbandhana-kumbhābhishēka of the (gods) Śrī Brahaṉnāyagi and Śrī
Kaṉakagirīśvaraṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with an obeisance to the God Bhavāni Śankara. Records
the consecration of the temple (Dēvāli) by Du(Tu)lajā rāja, Mahārājā sāḥeb Rāmaji
rāje sāheb. The date portion is not clear, probably mentions a cyclic year and 10th
Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 399 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Śrīraṅgadēva-mahāraya (Raṅga II) Year: Śaka 1499, Īśvara, Kumbha,
ba. 28. Sunday, dvitiya, Hasta = 22nd February 1578 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to record some grant for providing food offerings to the god,
Tirumalar-uḍaiyasvāmiyār at Dēvakkāpuram, a dēvasthāna in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu,
adjacent to Rājagambiraṉ-malai in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division)
of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, on the auspicious occasions in the months of Māsi and
Vaikāsi, for the merit of Agastiyappa-nāyakkar of Vēlūr. Other details are not clear.

edition.l: 14[ 90kku] mel cellā niṉṟa

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
This Sanskrit record is in Grantha characters of about the 16-17th century A.D.
It records that Īśānaśiva-Yōgindra, the head of the Gōlaki-maṭha, of Gāyatri-gōtrā
obeyed the command of Śiva.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: ⎯⎯ Year: Śaka 1401, Viḷambi, Mithuna
śu. di. ·7, Monday Uttiraṭṭādi, Subhayōga and Gājakarṇa =
22nd June 1478 A.D. The tithi was ba. 7 and not śu. 7.

inscription.p:
Incomplete and badly damaged. Records the dedication of a number of maid-
servants (dēvaḍimai) to the Periyanāchchiyār shrine (in the) Tirumalaināyinār (temple)
at Dēvakkāpuram, a tiruṉāmakkāṇi of the Tirumalar-uḍaiyār temple, a dēvasthānam
in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of
Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. These women were dedicated by Ettappanāyakkar for
the merit of Īśura-nāyakkar and the same matter was engraved on the kumudappaḍai
of the first tower in the Periyanāchchiyār temple.

Timmāyi, the daughter of Kaḷappāḷar, Veṉṟāl, the daughter of Veṅkaṭa, Mūttāḷ,
the daughter of Koḻundan and several others were dedicated to the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the second Gōpura, left of entrance, Bṛihadāmba temple, left of entrance.

p.b:
King: Narasiṅgarāya-uḍaiyār Year: Śaka 140[3] A.D. Viḷambi, Mithuna, śu. 7 Monday,
Uttiraṭṭādi, Subhayōga, Gajanāya karaṇa = This data may correspond to
22nd June 1478 A.D., if the Śaka date was 1400 and ba. di. 7.

inscription.p: Incomplete. Begins with the epithet of the king, Śrīmaṉmahāmaṇḍalēśura.

Refers to the territorial division Rājagembhīraṉ-malai in Murugamaṅgalapaṟṟu
in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the first gōpura, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.b:
King: Sadāśivadēva Mahārāyar Year: Śaka 1492, Kshaya, Kaṟkaṭa,
śu. 10. Friday, Uttiraṭṭādi = irregular.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Records that Āyam Aḍaikkalaṅgkāttār and Eṟappar and others
(endowed some gift) inorder to burn a twilight lamp, for the merit of Śrīmadunāyakkar
Kṛishṇappa Nāyakkaraiyyaṉ and Vēlūr Eṟa Bomma Nāyakkar, at the temple in
Dēvikkāpuram.

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the first gōpura, of Bṛihadāmba temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and slightly damaged. This inscription records the gift of the taxes
levied on looms for conducting offerings and worship (to the god of) Dēvakkāpuram
for the merit of Iliṅgamanāyakkar. It also records that this charity was instituted
during the days of (regime) Ēkāmbaranāyakkar, son of Rāmappanāyakkar. Other
details are not known as the purport is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Gaṇēśa temple at the fresh-water tank in the same village.
inscription.p:
Records that the tank and the temple were the gifts of one of the merchants
(nagarattar) of the Vaiśya-vāṇiyaṉ sect, Vaṇṇat Taragaṉ Taṇḍa-nayiṉār.

This record on the grounds of palaeography may be assigned to the
13th century A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1912.)
DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the gōpura, Kanakagirīśvara temple situated on a hill.

p.b: King: ⎯⎯ Date: Kali 4990; Śaka 1811, Virōdhi, Kārttigai 14, November = 27, 1888 A.D.

inscription.p:
In modern characters. Refers to one Abhinavapūrṇapriya Śrīnivāsa Rao Sāhēb,
Jagirdār of Ārṇi, piously performed the ceremony ashṭabhandhana-kumbhābhishēka
of Kanakagīrīśvara on this day, i.e., 27th November 1888 A.D.

edition.l: kaliyāptāha 4990
cakāha 1811
māsam 14 nāḷ kkuc
cariyāṉa 1888 m varuṣam
navampar mātam 27 ḷ yi-

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the south base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Begins with the meykīrtti of the ruler, tirumaṉṉi-vaḷara. It records that the
members of the ūr, Taṉkāḷi Pugaḻi, Dēvandai Sēndaṉ Saduran, Vaḍukantai Vaiyiri
Tāṅgi and Vaṇṇakkaṉ Sāttaṉ udaimaṉ gifted ninety sheep for burning a perpetual
lamp to the god of the temple for the merit of Veḷḷāḷaṉ Puttēri Kuṭṭattāḻi alias
Kalivēlippēraiyaṉ of Marudāḍu alias Vikramaśōḻanallūr in Marudāḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-
kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍalam.

It is further stated that the ūrār sold land to Uḻundaiy-uḍaiyāṉ Aiyyāṟaṉ
Bhaṭṭālagaṉ, an agent of the army official, (daṇḍanāyakan) Madhurāntakamārāyaṉ of
Marudāḍu alias Vikramaśōḻanallūr, for digging a tank for quenching the thirst
(śrīmadāham nīṅga) of the above individual Kalivēlippēraiyyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Kulōttuṅga Chōḷa I Year 32: 1102 A.D.

inscription.p:
Builtin. Records the gift of land, (measuring) two taḍi, after purchase, by
Sikkaluḍaiyāṉ Vēḷāṉ Vaḻuvānilaiyāṉ Vaḷavaṉ Muvē[ndavēḷāṉ] from the ūrār of
Marudāḍu alias Vikramaśōḻanallūr after paying both for the cost of the land and the
taxes accruing to the land. The individual then donated the same for the purpose of
providing food offerings to the god Umāśkanda ••••••Avanimuḻuḍai-nāyakkar.

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 409 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of sheep by Eṉādiyi Sūrāḍiyakaṇḍaṉ of Marudāḍu in Marudāḍu-
nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam, to burn a lamp in the big temple of Marudāḍu. The
donor entrusted the gift (sheep) to a shepherd, Mādēvaṉ, who agreed to measure out
5 nāḻi annually for burning the lamp.

kātt[ā]r

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 410 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the west base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Rājanārāyaṇan Sambhuvarāyar (II) Year 16, Vaikāśi, 1354 A.D.

inscription.p:
This epigraph records that during the sixth regnal year of the above ruler (1344
A.D.) the tank of the village, Marudāḍu alias Vikramachōḻanallūr in Marudāḍu-nāḍu
in Veṇkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, got breached
because of heavy floods caused by excessive rain. Consequently the sluice (kaliṅgu),
which regulated the excess water, was damaged and water flow to the nearby
villages, such as Koṭṭaiūr was stopped and it continued for nearly a decade. During
the 16th regnal year (of the king) efforts were made to supply water to Kōttaiūr by
excavating channels and raising the level of the embankment. Accordingly the ūrār
of Kōṭṭaiūr entered into an agreement with the ūrār of Marudāḍu and obtained the
right to dig channels by remitting 260 aṉrāḍu-naṟpaṇam. This inscription also details
how these channels were dug and the course of the flow of the water to different
tanks.

The account (of Marudāḍu) Malaikiṉiyaniṉṟāṉ Karikālachōḻa Brahmārāyaṉ and
others figure as signatories.

l.ta:
vatu vaikācimātattu oruṉāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattu veṇkuṉṟak koṭṭattu vātavūrṉāṭṭu koṭṭai
ūravaṟku immaṇṭalattu ik-

koṭṭattu marutāṭunāṭṭu marutāṭāṉa vikkiramacoḻanallūr

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 411 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records the gift of land for burning lamps as tiruviḷakkuppuṟam by the ūrār
of Marudāḍu to the temple of Peruntirkkōyil-āḻvār for the merit of Taḍaṅgan
Ayyaṉ Kalipperumān of Māgaṉūrp-pūttuṟai, who died so that the village Marudāḍu,
which was in Marudāḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟa-kōṭṭam, may not be destroyed (aḻiyāmai-
yaṉṟu).
This inscription suggests that both the ūrār and nāṭṭavar are the same local bodies.

l.ta:
a[y*]yaṉ kalipperumān eṅka(ḷ)ḷuraḻiyāmaiyanṟu
ceytuppaṭṭamaiyāl ivaṟkkuāka peru-

rukkoyillāḷvāru[k*]ku [vai]tta viḷakkuppuṟam melai ālañceṟuvu
viḷakkuppuṟam vaitto-
ceytār ceta pāvattai koḷvān itu rakṣit[t*][ā*]r śrī pātam en-

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Rājarājakēśarivarman, who destroyed Sālai (Rājarāja I) Year 12: 997 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of seventy sheep by Uma Nīli Rājarāja-aṇukka
Pallavaraiyan, to the god (āḷvār) of the big temple (peruntirukkōyil) at Marudāḍu in
Marudāḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkunṟa-kkōṭṭam.

l.ta: ma nili rājarājaaṇukkapal[la*]varaiyan viṭṭa āṭu eḻupatu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 413 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Vīra Narasiṅga Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1429, Kshaya, Paṅguṉi, 2,
Mīna, śu. 5, Attam, Sunday = Probably Sunday 29th March 1506 A.D. 2 Mesha, not
Mīna, and the star was Rōhini.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Contains only the birudas of the king and the date. Other details
are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 414 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Records a gift of a village Vāḍikuppam, a hamlet in Marudāḍu as tirunāmattukkāṇi
for conducting offerings and worship to the god (uḍaiyavar) of the big temple
(perun-tirukkōyil) at Marudāḍu in Vāttippaṟṟu, for the merit of Timmarāśasvāmi
Eṟṟamārāsa, an agent of Dēvachōḻa mahārāsa. (mistake for Dēvarāja Mahārāja ?)

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 415 of 1912.)
MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
On the north wall, maṇḍapa infront of the Purandarēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Achutadēva Mahārāya Date: Vijaya, Tai 1, Sunday śu.
12 Saṅkrama Puṇyakālam = 28th December 1533 A.D.

inscription.p:
In this inscription letters are engraved in bigger size. It registers a gift of two
villages Aṉṟilēri and Vāḍikkuppam to the god of the big temple at Marudāḍu, for
the merit of the king's accountant (rāyarkaraṇikam) Piḻaipoṟuttār piḷḷai and also
Tiruvēṅgaḍam-uḍaiyār piḷḷai, the son of Vaḍamalaiyār piḷḷai during the authority of
Tittaṉ. The accountant of the village Biramarāyaṉ, the ūrārs of Marudāḍu, and an
individual, Uttaṇḍan, figure as signatories. At the end record it wishes for the
welfare of preceptor (guru vāḻavēṇum).

l.ta:

paṣattu tiruttuvāteciyil c[e]ṅkirama puṇṇiyakāla
