uḷpaṭa iṟṟai nāḷ[ā*]-

l viṟṟukkuṭuttu koḷvatāṉa emmilicainta vilaipporuḷ aṉṟāṭu
naṟkācu

ikkācu patiṉaiyyāyiramum āvaṇakkaḷiye k[ā*]ṭṭeṟṟi
kaicelavaṟa koṇṭu viṟṟu vilaipirāmaṇaicaivutiṭṭu kuṭutto-

m nāyaṉ coṟaṉāṉa iruṅkoḷaṟku ūraṉ nampiyāvāṉ virametāvi
peraiyaṉu[m*] mallaṉ maṇavāḷaṉāna keṅkataraiyaṉum ivviruvom
ikkācu patiṉaiyyāyirattukkum innāṉkellaikku uḷpaṭṭa
naṉceykku[m]

puṉceykku[m] maṉaikkum toṭṭattukkum maṉṟum kaṉṟum
nilaippāḻukkum meṉokkiya maramum kiḻnokkiyakiṇaṟu maṟṟum
epperppaṭṭa pirāttikaḷum uḷpaṭa ikkācu patiṉaiyyāyiramume
vilaiyāvutākavum itu ve-

poruḷmāvaṟuti poruḷcelavolaiyāvutākavum ituvallatu
veṟuporuḷmāvaṟutip poruḷ [c*]elavolai
kāṭṭakkaṭavatallārākavum ippaṭi cammatittu vilaikkaṟaviṟṟu poruḷaṟa
koṇṭu viṟṟu [vi]-

lai piramāṇa icaivutiṭṭu kuṭuttom nāyan coṟaṉāṉa iruṅkoḷaṟku
ūraṉ na[m*]piyāṉa virametāviperaiyaṉum mallanmaṇavāḷaṉāna
koṅkataraiyaṉum ivviruvom ippaṭikku itu vaṭapāṭāvūruṭaiya
curitimāṉ [ū]ran • • mpi

yāṉa virametāviperaiyaṉ cayiñāta(ta)nmaikku ivvūṟ kāṇi
uṭaiya taṭṭāṉ vaḷḷal iḷamaitaṭṭāneḻuttu ippaṭiku(ku) itu
vaṭapāṭāvūrruṭaiya curutimāṉ mallaṉ maṇavāḷaṉāna koṅkataraiyaṉ
cayiñāṉatanmaikku ivai i[la]-

kciccāṉ taṇakkaṉ uṟakkatteḻuppi eḻuttu ivarkaḷ colla
innilavilaippiramāṇa icaivutiṭṭu eḻutinen nāṭṭukkaṇakku kuṟucci

[u]ṭaiyāṉ kū[ṭa]ttāṭ koṇṭāṉ aṟaṅkāttā neḻuttu
ippaṭikku ivai vaṭap[ā]ṭāvūrruṭai-

ya curutimā[n] •••viḻu •••nāyaneḻuttu ippaṭikku i[vai*]
vaṭapāṭāvūruṭaiya curitimāṉ ūrmaṇavāḷaṉāṉa c[o]ḷentira
ciṅkanāṭāḻvāṉukku te(ta)puttiraṉāṉa

ūraṉ coḷentira ciṅka
nāṭāḻvāṉ cayiñāṉataṉmaikku i[vai paṭu]-

vūruṭaiyāṉ [c]entāmarai kaṇṇaṉ tiruvekampamuṭaiyāṉ neḻuttu
ippaṭikku itu teṉpāṭāvūruṭaiya curutimā[n] vāḻavantā nāyaṉāna
cempiyan perai[ya]n cayiñāṉataṉmaikku ivūr taccanākanāṭakan
ci[ṅ]katevaneḻuttu

ippaṭi aṟiven teṉpāṭāvūruṭaiya curutimān koyil
ūṟaiyarāyaneḻuttu vaṭapāṭāvūruṭaiya curutimāṉ nampiciya[n*]
cayiñānatanmaikku ivvūrilirukkum vāṇikaril
coṉnavāṟaṟivāneḻuttu i[ru]kūruṭaiya curutimān viranmalai kalak-

kiyum tiruvan kaṅkai[ma]lai nāṭāḻvāṉum kāmaṉ vicaiyālaiya
nāṭāḻvāṉum immuvar cayiñāṉatanmaikku ivai toṇṭapara[mi]
uṭaiyāṉ tūṇanuṭaiya piḷḷai [e]ḻuttu itu irukūr uṭaiyān
perumāṉ periyanāṭṭu nampi cayiñāṉa tan-

inscription.head:
No. 501.
(A.R. No. 501 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west wall, first prākāra, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Rājarāja III Year: 16+1st Karkaṭaka, śu. di. 3
Wednesday, Pūśam = 21st July 1232 A.D.

inscription.p:
Registers the sale of 1(3/4) vēli, 2 mā of dry land, 15(1/2) vēli and 2 mā of wet
land, garden, house and grazing ground, by Aṟaiyan Mallan alias Birāmādarāya
Muttaraiyaṉ of Ūṟṟattūr in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu to his bodyguard (agambadiyār)
Śrutimāṉ Nāyaṉ Soṟaṉ alias Iruṇgōḷar of Neṟkuḷam, for 6000 naṟkāśu.

Uḍaiyaṉ Muttayattaṉ Nāṅgūr uḍaiyāṉ Varaṉṉi Āḻvāṉṉāyaṉ Śīmāhēśvara-vēḷāṉ,
the accountant of Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu, who wrote this document and several other figure
as signatories.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī irājarājatevaṟku
yāṇṭu patiṉāṟāvatiṉ etirāmā[ṇ*]ṭu kaṟkaṭakanā[ya*]ṟṟu

tiyaiyum putaṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pū[cattu nāḷ
vaṭakarai irājarāja vaḷa]nāṭṭu ūṟṟattūr nāṭṭaraiyaṉ mallaṉ
ciyaṉāṉa pi-

ramātarāya muttaraiyaṉeṉ [e]ṉ akam[pa*]ṭiyāril neṟkuḷamuṭaiya
curitimāṉ ṉāyaṉ coṟaṉāṉa iruṅkoḷaṉukku nilavilai[p*] piramā[ṇa*]
i(c)aivu tiṭṭu eṉ kāṇiyāy nāṉ viṟki[ṉ]ṟa vaṭapāṭāvūr
naṉcey puṉcey maṉaikkum ellaiyāvatu pāṇṭaneri eṉṟu per kū-

vappaṭṭa kuḷa[t]tukkum naṉceyviḷainilattukkum ellaiyāvatu
vaṭapāṟkellai kuṭamuruṭṭi kaṟaṭṭukku teṟku ayyaṉ pātti
meṭṭukku teṟkum puṟkuḷattu el[lai] caṭaiy[ā]ṇṭār [va]ṭṭattukkum
poṉṉāṇṭār vaṭṭattukku ūraṉ eṉ-

ṟa nilattu[k]ku teṟkum itaṉ meṟku kuḷattu nir k[o]val ayya[ṉār
ta]ṇakkāṇṭār koyilukkum itaṉ meṟku caṭuppa[r] kiṇaṟṟukkum
tāḻaikkuṭṭattu karaikkum [koyi] •••• kkum paṟaiccerikku

meṟkum āka icainta perunāṉkellai naṭuvu u[ṭ*]paṭṭa nilam
pati[ṉāla]ṭiyum vilakkaṭiyumāṉa [ko] •••

nilam itil eṉṉopāti nālil oṉṟu » orumā iti[l*]

ceyyā ivarkaḷ peṟṟuṭaiyā aṉupokittu varukiṟa pala taṭiyāl
rūpāy
nikki » mukkāl iraṇṭumā iṉṉilam mukkāle iraṇ-

ṭumāvum iṉṉāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭ[ṭa*] kaḷar iṟaṅkalum nirkovalum
uṭpa[ṭa]vum puṟkuḷattukkum naṉcey nilattuk[ku*]m ellaiyāvatu
kiḻp[ā]ṟkellai kuṭamuruṭṭi kaṟaṭṭukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkel[lai]
pāṇṭane[ri*]ellaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai ayyaṉār ta-

ṇakkāṇṭār koyilukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai puṟkuḷat[tu]

perunāṉke[llai na]ṭuvuṭ[pa]ṭṭa naṉc[e*]y patiṉālaṭiyum
vilakkaṭiyumāṉa kolāl uttecappaṭi naṉcey

li iṉṉilam veliyum

kiḻpāṟkellai pūṉaikaṟaṭṭukkum cūḷai-

ellaiyil peṟci kuḻikkum kaḷiyaṉkuṟicci ellaiyil kucavaṉ kuḻikkum
koḷkiṟāṉ kāṇiyāṉa neṭuṅkūr ellaikku vaṭakkum [m]elpāṟkellai

ḻikkuk kiḻakkum teṉ pāṭāvūr ellaiyil ūṟaiṉāṭāḻvāṉ pālan
ceykku kiḻakkum [vaṭa]pāṟkellai maṇiyaṉkuṟiccikku pokiṟa
pe[ru]vaḻikku teṟkum itaṉ kiḻakku aracaṉ pā[ḻi]karaiyil nakarattā[r*]
[ma]tuḷukkum pa[ṟ*]aiccerikkum teṟkum āka icainta peru nā • • •

ellai naṭuvupaṭa puṉceyyum kuḷamum uṭpaṭa patiṉāṟaṭiyum vilakkaṭiyum
āṉa kolāl mikutikkuṟaivu

itil
eṉṉopāti nālil oṉṟu paṭutaraṅkāṇi kuḷamum naṉcey • •
ttalā[m] nilamum ū[r*]ppuṉcey rūpāy

ṭu vaṭakku vaṭapāṭāvūr vuṭparappil puṉcey [nilat]tukku ellaiyāvatu
kiḻpāṟkellai [para]malai ellaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟ[kellai] ayyaṉār
taṇakkāṇṭār koyilukku kiḻakkum [ṭa] pāl •••• kku vaṭakkum
mel[pā]ṟkellai teṉpāṭāvūr ellaikku kiḻakku vaṭapāṟkellai

kūmpaṉ ellaikku teṟkum āka icainta (perunāṉku) perunāṉkellai
naṭuvuṭpaṭ[ṭa*] puṉcey utteca[m] » • vuli itil eṉṉopāti
nālil oṉṟu

liyum maṉaikkum toṭṭattukkum ellaiyāvatu
melpāṟkellai nakarattār matuḷukku kiḻakkum

vaṭapāṟkellai teṉpāṭāvūr ellaiyil ellaik kallukku teṟkum
itaṉ kiḻakku teṉpāṭāvūr ellai vaṉṉiteventirap pallavaraiyar
maṉaikku teṟkum kiḻpāṟkellai vaṭakku nokki pokiṟa teruvil

inscription.head:
No. 502.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the north wall, first prākāra, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription is carelessly engraved with a lot of scribal errors and also much
damaged at the end. It begins with the historical introduction of the king,
Pūmaruviya poḻilēḻum etc., It seems to record a gift of land, probably after purchase
by Śrutimāṉ Muvāyirattoruvaṉ Jananāthan alias Edrilisōḻa Nāḍāḻvāṉ, for celebrating
a festival called Ūrōsaṇi-ttirunāḷ (asterism of Rōhiṇi) at the temple. Other details are
lost.

inscription.head:
No. 503.
(A.R. No. 503 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the same wall, first prākāra, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Kulōttuṅga III Year 16, Maka; śu. di. 7
Friday, Rēvati = 1st January 1194 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. This epigraph records the sale of dry and wet lands, excempted from
taxes, by the authorities (Ādichaṇḍēśvara) of Togumāmaṇi Nāyaṉār temple at
Ūṟṟattūr in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, to a dancing-girl (dēvaraḍiyāḷ)
Umaiāḻviyāḷ Kāravallanaṅgai of the above temple, who consecrated the image of the
god, Umai-Iśvaram uḍaiya nāyanār at Tiruneḍuṅgūr, a dēvadāna of the temple. It
appears the land was sold to meet the expenses for the construction of tirumaḍaiviḷāgam,
(residential quarters around the temple), flower garden and other expenditure connected
with the offerings and worship of the temple. Among the boundaries of the land
sold reference is made to a Piḍāri temple. The temple authorities having sold the
land received the amount [kaḻañju] and probably deposited the same for the
abhishēka ceremony. Rest of the details is damaged.

vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ
muṭittalaiyumkoṇṭaruḷi[ya] śrīkolottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
ti nāḷ vaṭakarai irājarāja vaḷanāṭṭu ūṟṟattū[r*]-

inscription.head:
No. 504.
(A.R. No. 504 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south base, Akhilāṇḍēśvari shrine, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to Kulōttuṅga III. Because in another inscription (No.
522) of this king the same dancing-girl figures as a donor. It records the consecration
of the image, goddess (Nāchchiyār) and also the construction of the temple in stone
and wall around the temple (tirumadiḷ) by Umaiāḻvi Tāiyilunallāḷ alias
Śivakāmasundarimāṇikkam, a dancing girl (dēvaraḍiyāḷ) of uḍaiyār Togumāmaṇi-
nāyanār temple.

inscription.head:
No. 505.
(A.R. No. 505 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Akhilāṇḍēśvari shrine, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Vīrarājēndrachōḷa, who was pleased take Madurai ie., Kulōttuṅga III Year ••••••

inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. It seems to record that Poṉnamallaṉ alias Brahmādarāya
Muttaraiyan and a lady (name not given) conferred the right of the performance of
worship (Śivabrāhmaṇakāṇi) in the goddess (Nāchchiyār) temple (along with the
brāhmaṇas of thirty circle) on a certain Sōdiperiyanāyan alias Villavan Bhaṭṭan. It
is stated that the latter was the priest (Śivabrāhmaṇar) of the uḍaiyār temple.

l.ta:
ttu poṉnamalla[ṉ*] āṉa piramātarāyamuttaraiyanen uṭaiyār
koyil civapirāmaṇaril kā ••••••••••

ppiyāṉ coti periyanāyan āṉa villavanpaṭṭanukku
ittirukkāmakkoṭṭa nācciyārkoyil civappirāmaṇakkā[ṇi*]
muppatu vaṭ[ṭa*]-

mum tanmatānamāka nānum ivaḷum nirvāttu kāṇiyākak kuṭuttom
ivilla
l.note: There is a space of about 5 inches between villa and paṭṭaṉuk

inscription.head:
No. 506.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Āṇḍavar shrine, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Kulōttuṅga chōḷa, who was pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya.
Year 24, Mēsha, śu. di. 4, Rōsaṇi = Friday, 29th March 1202 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription records a gift of land by a maid servant (agambaḍi-
peṇḍugaḷ) of the temple, Periya Nāchchi-uḍaiyār Nāchchi of Āṟagaḷūr in Āṟṟū-
kūṟṟam in Vaḍakarai Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, to the god uḍaiyār Tugumāmaṇi-nāyanār of
Ūṟṟattūr in the same nāḍu, for providing offerings (tiruppaḍimāṟṟu) to the saints
(muttirattu-nāyaṉār). It seems that the gifted land, both wet and dry, as dēvadāna was
bought from two individuals of Śrīkaṇṭa-chatūrvēdimaṅgalam for five hundred and
fifty kāśu. The inscription also contains details on some land transactions but it is not
clear whether they are related to the present grant or not, on account of the
damaged nature of the record.

inscription.head:
No. 507.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Āṇḍavar shrine, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Tribhuvanavīradēva ie., Kulōttuṅga III
Year 36, Dhanus, śu. di. 3, Monday Tiruvōṇam = 16th December 1213 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. Seems to register a sale of land by the authorities (Ādichaṇḍēśvara)
of Togumāmaṇi nāyaṉār temple at Ūṟṟattūr in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu to two individuals,
Vēḷāḷaṉ Aṇḍakkuḍaiyan Sōḍaṉ Siṟṟaṉ and Āvarlūr-uḍaiyān Tirumaḻumān Maṇavāḷaṉ.
It appears that the land was purchased by a certain Periyanāchchiyār from a
brāhmaṇa lady (residing) at Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and donated to the temple,
which in turn sold it to the above persons. Periyanāchchiyār is described as
agambaḍi-peṇḍugaḷ of the temple (see last no. 506).

inscription.head:
No. 508.
(A.R. No. 508 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Āṇḍavar shrine, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Jaṭāvarman Sundarapāṇḍya I Year 18, Tulā, śu. di. 10,
Monday, Sadaiyam = 23rd October 1262 A.D.

inscription.p: This inscription commences with the historical introduction Samasta jagadādhāra etc.

It records the nāṭṭavar of Kuṉṟa-kkūṟṟam-nāḍu in Uttaṅgatuṅga-vaḷanāḍu met in
full quorum at the hall, called Uttamaśōḻaṉ-tirumaṇḍapam of Togumāmaṇi-nāyaṉar
temple at Tiruūṟṟattūr in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. They donated the
villages Iḍaimalai and Veṭṭuṇi, as tax-free, to a certain Āyaṉ Kavi Kumārar. It is
stated that these villages were left fallow and its tanks were ruined for a long time
and they were now entrusted to the donee to reclaim the forests, repair the tank and
cultivate the lands. This grant was also engraved on the (walls of the) temple of
Togumāmaṇi-nāyaṉār temple. Pulimiśaiyūr-uḍaiyāṉ Dēvaṉ Kāliṅgarāyaṉ, Kiḷiyūr-uḍaiyāṉ
Vīragaṅgaṉ, Sāttanūr-uḍaiyāṉ Gurukularāyaṉ and others including the (silpāchāriyam)
Āgāravallabha-Āchāriyaṉ and Nāṭṭāchāriyaṉ figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 509.
(A.R. No. 509 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west wall, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription commences with the meykīrtti of the king, Pūmālai-miḍantu
etc., It records that the authorities (represented by) Togumaṇiyār Chaṇḍīśan of the
Mahādēvar temple at Tiruvūṟattūr in Tribhuvanamuḻuḍai-vaḷanāḍu sold four vēli of
land, (at Ukkalūr) to a certain Arumaṇi-uḍaiyān alias Poṟkōyil Pichchaṉ a veḷḷāḷa of
Ūṟṟattūr for 90 kāśu, for maintaining a maṭha, (maḍappuṟam) (which was perhaps
situated in the temple premises). It is also enjoined upon the buyer to settle the kuḍi
(cultivators) of his choice to get the land cultivated and measured out ten kalam of
paddy daily to maintain the (servants) who cleaned the premises of the maṭha
(called) Kēraḷāntakan-maḍam and also feed mendicants there. Further it is recorded
that the sale amount of ninety kāśu was given to the buyer, Poṟkōyil Pichchaṉ who
remitted an interest of 67(1/2) kaḻañji of gold, which was utilized for making paṭṭam
(headgear ornament), called Kēraḷāntakan-paṭṭam to the god. It is stated that this was
to adorn the god daily. Further both i.e., the maṭha and Kēraḷāntakan-paṭṭam were
to be protected by the nāṭṭavar of Ūṟṟattūr, who agreed to maintain both these
charities and the temple authorities gave an agreement to Poṟkōyil Pichchaṉ.

The accountant of the nāḍu Kuṉichcha uḍaiyān Togumāmaṇi Tiruchchiṟṟambalam
uḍaiyān, Kaviśikan Chakrapāṇi and several others figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 510.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west wall, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Built in. Begins with the meykīrtti of the king, Pūmādu puṇara etc; Seems to
register a gift of 3(1/4) vēli of land as ūr-kīḻiṟaiyili by Uttamaśōḻaṉ alias Virāṭarājaṉ,
a resident of Ādamaṅgalam, for conducting various rituals in the temple during the
month of Chittirai.

inscription.head:
No. 511.
(A.R. No. 511 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the same wall, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Parakēsarivarman Tribhuvanachakravatti Vikramachōḷa Year 13: 1131 A.D.

inscription.p:
Begins with the meykīrtti of the king Pu-mādu etc; of the ruler. It records a sale
of land by Bāradāyaṉ Kūttaṉ alias Kailāśam-uḍaiyāṉ, a brāhmaṇa of Śrikaṇṭa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēyam of Tiruvūṟṟattūr-nāḍu (a sub-division) of
Tribhuvanamuḻuḍai-vaḷanāḍu, for ten aṉṟāḍu-naṟkāśu to the god uḍaiyār
Togumaṇiyāṇḍār of Ūṟṟattūr. It is stated that the seller obtained this land called
Paṟaichchey, by way of dowry (strīdhanam) from a certain Vasishṭaṉ Araśuvaṉ
Sīyaṉāthan (his father-in-law). Among the boundaries, of the land sold, a common
land endowed for the livelihood of vēdic brāhmaṇas (kiḍaippuṟam) is mentioned.

jayam[ā*]tu virumpa taṉ tiruppatumalar maṉna-

var cūṭa maṉṉiyavurimaiyāl maṇimu[ṭi]cūṭi ceṅkol ceṉṟu

inscription.head:
No. 512.
(A.R. No. 512 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west wall, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Built in on the left portion of the record. Begins with the meykīrtti of the king
[Pu-mādu pu]ṇara etc.,

Records that the members of the mūlasabhā of Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
deposited ten kāśu in the treasury, which they obtained as price for the land they
sold to a certain Āridan Sōlai Perumāṉ. It is stated that land was obtained by the
sabhā through confiscation, originally belonged to a brāhmaṇa, Vasiṭṭan Tiruchchiṟṟam
•••••, a resident of Ūṟṟattūr in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu, (a sub-division of) Tribhuvaṉamuḻuḍai-
vaḷanāḍu, perhaps a priest in the temple at Ūṟṟattūr. The said priest had committed
a minor offence in the Uḍaiyār Tugumāmaṇiyāṇḍār temple of the above village and
he was fined 20 aṉṟāḍu-naṟkāśu. Besides the priest was unable to pay the taxes on
his land at Paṟaichchēri, a locality in Śrīkaṇṭa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam. Hence his land
was seized and half of it (sembādi) was sold by the mūlasabhā.

vi[ru]mpa taṉ tiruppatumalar maṉṉavar cūṭa maṉ • •

l.note: Read as camaintu. This record is incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 513.
(A.R. No. 513 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west base, yāga-maṇḍapa, Marundīśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Parakēśarivarman alias Rājādhirājadēva I, who destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷūr-sālai.
Year 28: 1046 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete and built in. The title ‘Parakēsarivarman’ is obviously a mistake for
Rājādhirāja I who bore the title Rājakēsarivarmaṉ. This inscription records the sale
of lands, belonging to Kauśikan Kaṇḍaṉ Ambalattāḍi, Kaṇḍaṉ Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ, Nīlakaṇṭaṉ
Aiyāṟaṉ, and Kodaṇḍaṉ Pāṇḍaṉ, which had been in arrears (of land tax) for a long
time, (as they migrated to other country), to the god of Tiruvūṟattūr, for 16 kaḻañju
of gold.

l.ta:
•••••••••• tūr nāṭṭu tiruvūṟṟattūr mūla •••tiruvūṟṟattūr
perumāṉaṭikaḷukku eṅ •••• rāmaṇi kocikaṉ kaṇṭaṉ
ampalattāṭi nilamu[m*] kaṇṭaṉ tirunilakaṇṭaṉ nilamum
nilakaṇṭa(ṇṭa)ṉ ai
l.note: The letter ai is written ḍa

yyāṟaṉ nilamum kotaṇṭan pāṇṭaṉ
nilamu[m*] neṭuṅkālam iṟaiyirutu

ivarkaḷ nilam kuḷamuṅ kuḷakkaḻaniyum ilmaṉai
toṭṭam puṟaṇiyum vilaikku viṟṟu koṇṭa poṉ patiṉaṟu kaḻañ
•••ppon koṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 514.
(A.R. No. 514 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west base, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This incomplete inscription, which is engraved in late characters, begins with
the usual meykīrtti of the ruler, Tirumagaḷ-pōla etc., of the above king. It seems to
record some dealing between two persons, Vēḷmuttaraiyaṉ and Perumāṉ Tiruveṇṇāvallāṉ
Mullaiarai [Peru]muttaraiyaṉ. Other details are lost.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷpolap peru[ṉi]lac celviyuṉ taṉakke urimaippūṇṭamaiy
maṉakkoḷa kāṉtaḷūrcālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkai ṉāṭuṅ kaṅ-

ka pāṭi ṉuḷampapāṭiyuṉ taṭikaiy vaḻiyuṅ kuṭamalai nāṭuṅ kollamuṅ
kaliṅkamum eṇṭicai pukaḻ īḻamaṇṭalamu iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai
ilakkamu-

veṉṟa ••••• k koṇṭu [tan]neḻil vaḷaruḻi ūḻi ellāyāṇṭuṉ
toḻutakaiy viḷaṅkum yāṇṭey ceḻiyarait tecukoḷ

ṭāṇ •••••••••• māṉ puḷḷiyā[ṇperiya]
veḷmuttaraiyaṉukku perumāṉ tiruveṇṇāvallāṉ mullai[raipe]ru
muttaraiyaṉ ṟaṇo

inscription.head:
No. 515.
(A.R. No. 515 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west base, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Incomplete and damaged. Seems to refer to the death of a certain Śrutimāṉ
Nakkaṉ Chandiraṉ alias Rājamalla Muttaraiyaṉ, a resident of Tiruvūrattūr in
[Ūṟ]ṟattūr-nāḍu (a sub-division) of Viḻā-nāḍu. He was ordered by the king (perumāḷ
tiruvāy-moḻiya) to attack Satayāśraya [the western Chālukya king ?] and to pierce the
latter's elephant. This soldier apparently lost his life in the encounter, a gift was
made for his merit to the temple of Mahādēva at Tiruvūṟṟattūr.

l.ta:
•••• [u]ṟṟattūr[n][ā*]ṭṭut tiruvūṟṟattūr i[ru]ntu vāḻuñ
curutimāṉ nakkaṉ cantiraṉāṉa irājamal[la*] muttaraiya-

•••• vaṉaic cātti tampi nakkaṉ •••••••••• tiruvūṟṟattūr
mā

l.note:
Incomplete. By the side of the above inscription one more record is found engraved. It is fragmentary
and in characters of the eleventh century A.D.

2 coḻa[t]evaṟku ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 516.
(A.R. No. 516 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the west base, yāga-maṇḍapa, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Rājakēsarivarmaṉ alias Uḍaiyār Śrirājārājadē[va] who took the head of
Vīra Pāṇḍya and destroyed the ships at Kāndaḷūr-sālai. Year: ⎯⎯

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Stone missing at the end. Records a gift of a lamp to the temple
of Mahādēva at [Ūṟ]ṟattūr, for the merit of Surumāḍavichchiōsai Peṟṟāḷ, residing at
Tiraṇi in Vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
hātevaṟku vaḷanāṭṭu tiraṇi uṭaiya curumāṭavicci
ocai peṟṟāḷukku vaitta viḷakku oṉṟāl pa

inscription.head:
No. 517.
(A.R. No. 517 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged in the middle. Begins with the historical introduction of the ruler,
pū-maruviya etc.,

Records the gift of four cows by Tiruveṇkāḍu-uḍaiyāṉ alias
Abhimānamērukkoṟṟakkuḍai Kōn, a resident of Viśalūr in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu, for
burning a twilight lamp to the god of Ūṟṟattūr in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. Three
Śivabrāhmaṇas, who received these cows agreed to burn the said lamp at night.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirum[ātu]m puvimātuñ jayamā[tu]m
nāmaruviya kalaimātum pūkaḻmātum

tu pulka āruma •••ti neṟiyanaittum ta[ḻ]aippa varumuṟai
yurimaiyil maṇimuṭi

cūṭit tiṅkaḷ veṇ •••• kkeḷikaṟaṭṭu ••••••nikkūṭattānena

vaḷanāṭṭu [tiru]na[ṟai]yūr [n]āṭṭu vicalūr kiḻa •••• cca [u]ṭaiyān
tiruveṇkāṭuṭaiyānāna a-

viṭṭa pacu nālu i[p]-

mātevan tirucciṟṟampala-

kāṇiyuṭaiya civappi

maṇaṉ kārkkiyaṉ periyāṉ kaṇavatiyāṉ k[āk]kunāyaka paṭṭaṉ
kaikkoṇṭa pacu oṉṟum ikko-

yil kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇaṉ kārkki ••••• periyāṉāṉa
uttamacoḻapaṭṭaṉ kaikkoṇṭa

pacu oṉṟu[m] ā •••cu nālu ippacu ṉāluṅ kaikkoṇṭu iravu
oru santiviḷakku erikka

inscription.head:
No. 518.
(A.R. No. 518 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Kullōttuṅga Chōḻa III Year 7: 1185 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of five cows in order to burn a perpetual lamp
to the god. Three persons, Tirunāmattūkkāṇi Kaṟpāḍinanda Paguvāyaṉ Vīra Śōḻakōṉ
received two cows, Puttuḍaipūraṉ Kōḍaṉ Mūvāyirattuoruvaṉ received a cow and
Bhaṭṭan Perukandāṉ received two cows for supplying ghee for the lamp.

edition.l: 7- vatu tirununtāviḷakkukku ney aḷakka pacu koṇṭa

inscription.head:
No. 519.
(A.R. No. 519 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
Commences with the introduction Samastabhuvanāśraya etc., Records an order
issued by the ruler to Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvarkanmigaḷ and kōyilkaṇakku (temple
accountant) and the authorities of Togumāmaṇi nāyanār temple, at Ūṟṟattūr in
Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu (a sub-division) of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. As per this order the king
assigned 921 kalam of paddy at the rate of a kalam of paddy per mā of land,
realiseable from two villages, owned by the temple (in-nāyanār dēvadānam = āṉa
ūrgaḷ), Śiṟṟalakaikkuḍi and Arindamanallūr (situated) in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, for conducting
a service for the merit of his grandson, Visvanāthadēvaṉ. It is also recorded that the
endowment was to take effect from the rainy season (kār) of the 27th regnal year
of the king.

l.gr:
svastiśrī [||*] samasabhuva[nv]āśradha śrīprithvavalvabha malārājādhirāja
paramēśvara[dv]āravati pūravarādhīśvara yādava-

malarāja rāja
malapurāḷugaṇḍa kadana pracaṇḍa gaṇḍa bhēruṇḍa nēka[ṅ]ga

ṇḍya diśāpaṭṭa cōḷarājya pratiṣṭhā [cāryya] nissaṅga
pratāpa cakrava[rtti] pōśaḷa śrīvirasōmēśvaradēva

l.ta:
vaḷanāṭṭu ūṟṟattūrnāṭṭu ūṟṟattūr uṭaiyār
tokumāmaṇināyanār koyil āti

vaḷanāṭṭu innāyanār tevatānam āṉa ūrkaḷ
māk[kalattukku]t tiṟappil iṟuttu varum

ciṟṟaḷakaikkuṭi mākkalattāl nellu aṟunūṟṟunāṟkalamum
arintamanallūr mākkalattil ne-

llu muṉṉūṟṟu orupatteḻu kalane kuṟuṇiyum irupatteḻāvatu kār
mutal iṭṭom ivvāṇṭu

kār mutal kaikkoṇṭu intac canti mākkalattāl vanta uṭal
koṇṭu pūcaikkut tāḻvu paṭāma-

inscription.head:
No. 520.
(A.R. No. 520 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, right of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Dēvarāya-Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1343, Sārvarin, Tai, 1421 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Seems to record that Aṇṇāmalai-nāthar, the son of Mahaprādāni
(Naraśilupayaraśar) made some gift to the god Tugumāmaṇi nayinār. A certain
Āvuḍaiyāṉ is mentioned.

edition.p: 4. In continuation there are some unintelligible letters which may be the sign manual of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 521.
(A.R. No. 521 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, central shrine Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Kullōttuṅga Chōḻa III, who was pleased to take Madurai and the crowned head of Pāṇḍya.
Year 21: Kumbha, ba. di. 10, Thursday, Viśāka = 24th December 1198 A.D. (Makara and not Kumbha).

inscription.p:
Built in. Records that (the members of) nāḍu and nagaram of Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu
assembled at the Uttamaśōḻamaṇḍapa (situated) in the temple of Togumāmaṇi-
nāyaṉār and ceded the village Veṇmaṇippāḍi in Kīḻvaḷḷuvappāḍi-nāḍu and converted
it into a mercantile town (nagaram) called Tāyilunallāpuram, for the merit of
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa Vāṇakōvaraiyar. It is also stated that the nāṭṭār of seventy-nine nāḍus
got this transaction engraved on stone. The accountant of the nāḍu figures as the
signatory.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīmaturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
miyum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa vicākattu nāḷ vaṭakarai

iruntu innāṭṭu kiḻ vaḷḷuvappāṭi
nāṭṭu veṇmaṇippāṭiyaik kulot[tu*]ṅka coḻavāṇa kovaraiyar
perāl tāyilunallapuram enṟu nakaram eṟa ••••

ṭavum innakarattu eṟṟaccurukkañ ceytu • • ṇṭākil • [ā]ka
veyiruntu aṟacceyyakkaṭavatākavum coḻa •••ippaṭikku
kalveṭṭiviccom eḻupattoṉpatu nāṭṭo[m*]

• • ṭṭuk kaṇa[kku] mālavāyuṭaiyār kaṇṇuṭaiyāṉ
aṇukkāṇṭāṉ eḻuttu u ••••••••••

• • ṉāṭṭu kaṇar[ku]ṟicci uṭaiyāṉ tokumāmaṇi taṭuttāṭ
koṇṭāṉ eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 522.
(A.R. No. 522 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b: King: Vīrarājēndra i.e., Kullōttuṅgachōḷa III Year 5: 1183 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of four golden ornaments weighing together 220
1/2 kaḻañju, 2 mañjāḍi and 4 mā of gold. They consisted of upper garment
(tiru-uttiriyam), amulet (tirumāṅgalya kārigai), an arch doorway resembling a crocodile
(makara tōraṇam) and belt of gold (tiruppaṭṭigai), by Tayinallaḷ alias
Śivakāmasundarimāṇikkam, daughter of Umaiāḻvi alias Āgāravallanaṅgai, the dēvaraḍiyāḷ
of the god Tugumāmaṇi-nāyaṉār.

l.ta: ṭu mañcāṭiyu nālu mā it ti[ru]-

inscription.head:
No. 523.
(A.R. No. 523 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Rājakēsarivarman Tribhuvanachchakkaravatti Kullōttuṅga Chōḷa II
Year 8: 1141 A.D.

inscription.p: This inscription begins with the meykrītti, Pūmaṉṉu padumam etc.,

This inscription records that Saṇḍēsan, (the authority) of the Togumāmaṇiyāṇḍār
temple at Tiruvūṟṟattūr in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu, assigned half-a-vēli of land in two pieces to
a certain Śrutimān Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyān Jananāthan alias Aṇukkanambi, (a
native) of Kuṟichchi to supply one padakku of paddy to meet the daily expenses of
worship (tiruppaḍimāṟṟu) to the god, Kshētrapāla-piḷḷaiyār. This assignment was in
exchange of some land given to and ten kāśu for making ornaments, deposited with
the temple by the above person Śrutimān Aṇukkanambi.

This accountant of the nāḍu Togumāmaṇi Tiruchchiṟṟambalam-uḍaiyān, Kauśikan
Periyanambi Vichchādiran, Kauśikan Togumāmaṇi Uyyavandār and several others
figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 524.
(A.R. No. 524 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the second gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.

p.b:
King: Vīrapratāpa Achyutadēva Mahārāya. Year: Śaka 1463, Śārvarin
Kaṉṉi, śu. di. 10 = 15th September 1541 A.D. But the cyclic year was Pḷava.

inscription.p:
Damaged at the end. The king is endowed with a string of Sāḷuva birudas,
em-maṇḍalamum madikka, Īḻam tiṟai-koṇḍāṉ, Navakōḍinārāyaṇaṉ, Sōmakulatilakaṉ,
Pātāḷabhayaṅkaraṉ etc.,

It records that a certain Birāmi Nachchi Nāyakkar, son of Raṅgappa Nāyakkar,
royal official (śvāmi-ūḻiyam), issued a charitable deed through which he donated forty
poṉ to the temple treasury for conducting special offerings and worship, on the
occasion of the car festival, to the god Tuyyamāmaṇi-nayiṉār, for the merit of the
ruler Achchudaiyadēva-mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 525.
(A.R. No. 525 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the main gōpura, right of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This epigraph is in the 13th century characters. It begins with the order of the
king to the nāṭṭār of Uṟṟattūr-nāḍu to collect taxes, i.e., vāram and varisai. It is stated
that the tax kaḍamaipaṟṟu was to be collected as usual as in the previous years.
However, exemptions of taxes, applicable to dēvadānam [temple land], tiruvīḍaiāṭṭam
[lands of Śiva temple], paḷḷichchandam [lands of Jain temples], iyyanpaṭṭi [lands of
the village god], maḍappuram [lands of the maṭha], agarappaṟṟu (lands of the
brahmadēya) and bhaṭṭavritti [lands of the brahmaṇa priests] were to be continued
as in the previous years.
Ōrūruḍaiyāṉ and Vāṇadarāyaṉ figure as signatories.

l.ta:
vatti koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ
ūṟṟattūr nāṭṭu nāṭṭāṟku taṅkaḷ nāṭu muppattumūṉṟāvatu

etirāmāṇṭu mutal vāramum varicaiyuṅ koḷvatākac coṉṉom
ippaṭi ceyyumiṭattu katirppayir vārappaṟṟu vāra-

inscription.head:
No. 526.
(A.R. No. 526 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the main gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This record is in characters of the 12-13th century A.D. It begins with the order
of (certain official) Vattarāyaṉ conveying the grant of wet and dry land, freed from
taxes, as kāṇi to Arayakaḷ. It is also recorded that the grantees i.e., Arayakaḷ was
given the right to engrave the gift on stone.
Arasan Vattarāyan, the official signs at the end of this record.

l.ta: kkuk kallum veṭṭi koḷvate ivai aracan

l.note:
Below this another incomplete inscription which runs as follows:
1 svastiśrī [||*] tribhuvaṉaccakravatti koṉeriṉmai

inscription.head:
No. 527.
(A.R. No. 527 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the main gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription records the fixing of annual tax on cattle i.e., ten kāśu per
sheep, thirty kāśu per cow and hundred kāśu per buffalo in the eight sub-divisions
of Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu, Mēlvaḻi-nāḍu, Kīḻvaḷi-nāḍu, Vembar-nāḍu, Vaḍavaḻi-nāḍu, Vaḷḷuvappāḍi-
nāḍu, Karikālakanna-vaḷanāḍu, Pulivalappaṟṟu and Āmūr-nāḍu. It is also ordered that
the cattle, belonging to the Togumāmaṇi-nāyaṉār temple, were exempted from this
tax as per the old custom.

l.ta:
ūṟṟattūrnāṭṭu melvaḻināṭu kiḻvaḻināṭu vempārnāṭum
vaṭavaḻināṭum vaḷḷuva-

inscription.head:
No. 528.
(A.R. No. 528 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the main gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
It is in the 15th century characters. Purport is not very clear. Seems to refer to
the settlement of a dispute, which arose on account of the death of certain
Kāṅgayaṉ, who was holding some office. Two persons Sūrāṇḍār and Ariyāṇḍār are
stated to have settled the dispute by paying ten poṉ to Nayiṉān who staked his
claim to this position.
It is also recorded that Vīrappa Āchāri and Narasiṉāgaṉ wrote this record.

inscription.head:
No. 529.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the main gōpura, left of entrance, Siddharatnēśvara temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription is in characters of the 15-16th century A.D. Records the gift of
the door and of the paved floor by Ēṟampettināyakkar, son of Nāṟaṇppanāyakkar.

inscription.head:
No. 530.
(A.R. No. 530 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Chōḷēśvara temple, right of entrance.

p.b: King: Parakēśarivarman Rājarāja II Year: 4, day 126: 1150 A.D.

inscription.p: Begins with the usual meykīrtti of the king, Pūmaruviya poḻilēḻum etc.,

This inscription records an order issued by the king to the trustees (sthānattār)
of Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-Īśvaram-uḍaiyār temple at Ūṟṟattūr in Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu, assigning
the (land) tax, forty four māḍai collected from the village Uḻuttampāḍi, in Kāṉakkiḷiyūr-
nāḍu, as dēvadāna-iṟaiyili, for conducting offerings and worship to the god
Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-Īśvaram-uḍaiyār.

This inscription also records those officials of the land revenue department
(puravuvari) who took cognizance of this arrangement, i.e., Koṟṟamaṅgalam-uḍaiyān,
Śiṟupaṉṟūr-kiḻavaṉ Pullāli-uḍaiyāṉ, Siṅgaḷarājaṉ and others, who also figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 531.
(A.R. No. 531 of 1912.)
ŪṬṬATTŪR, LALGUDI TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Chōḷēśvara temple, right of entrance.

p.b:
King: Rājakēśarivarman alias Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Kulōttuṅgachōḻa II
Year: 13, 124th day: 1146 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Commences with the meykīrtti of the king, Pu-maṉṉu padumam
etc.,

It records the assignment of land tax (kāṇikkaḍaṉ) of one hundred and thirty-
three and odd māḍai collected from the village, Śrīvāḷappūr in Kāṉakkiḷiyūr-nāḍu,
by the ruler to expend for conducting offerings and worship to the god Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-
Īśvaram-uḍaiyār. It is stated that the god was consecrated by a certain Vichchādira-
nāḍāḻvāṉ, the younger brother of Brahmādarāya Muttaraiyaṉ, a resident of Ūṟṟattūr.
It is said that the former consecrated the god at the latter village which was in
Ūṟṟattūr-nāḍu (a sub-division) of Bhuvaṉamuḻuḍai-vaḷanāḍu. It is further stated that
village Śiṟuvāḷāppūr was ceded as dēvadāna-iṟaiyili for the above purpose.

The supervisory officials of land revenue department, Maṇakkuḍaiyān Kuṉṟaṉ-
kiḻāṉ, Neḍumaram-uḍaiyān and the royal secretary (tirumandira-ōlai) Aṉapāya
Mūvēndavēḷāṉ figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 532.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Damaged. In characters of the 12-13th centuries. Registers gift of 1(3/4) kāśu by
Vāḻavantāṉ Bhaṭṭaṉ for a lamp. Other details are lost.

l.ta:
•••••••••• nta viḷa •••••••••• k koṉṟukkum koyiṟ
kāṇiyuṭaiya na

inscription.head:
No. 533.
(A.R. No. 533 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravartti Rājarājadēva (III)
Year 7, Tulā [2]6, ba. di. [2] Kārttigai, Sunday = 23 October 1222 A.D.

inscription.p:
States that Aruḷāḷa-bhaṭṭan Tiruvēṭṭai Aḻagiyān, of Bhāradvāja [gōtra],
a priest of temple at Tirunīrmalai in Surattūr-nāḍu, in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam
(a sub-division of) Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, received 1(1/2) paḻaṅkāśu from
Kaikaiyarāyaṉ Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiyāṉ of Malaiyambākkam in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu
of the same division. The gift was made in order to burn a perpetual lamp
at the temple.

l.ta:
ttu puliyuṟkoṭṭattu curattūrnāṭṭu tirunirmalai emperumāṉukku
ikkoṭṭattu māṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu (ma)-

malaiyampākkattu kaiykaiyarāyan tiruvakatticuramuṭaiyān vaitta
cantiviḷakku oṉṟukku ik-

l.note: The letter ṇ is engraved below the line.

inscription.head:
No. 534.
(A.R. No. 534 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Rājarāja (III) Year 16, 40th day, 1232 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Registers a gift of ten vēli of land in two pieces as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
(including all the taxes) for providing food offerings and for maintaining perpetual
lamps during the midnight (?) service called Tiruvāḻipparappināṉ-sandhi in the
temple of Tirunīrmalai Emberumāṉ by Madurāntakap-pottappichchōḻaṉ Gaṇḍagōpālaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 535.
(A.R. No. 535 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the east wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravartti Year 6, Mīna, Monday, Urōsaṇi =
Rājarāja (III) 1222 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of two twilight lamps for the god at Tirunīrmalai
by (an official) Kaṇṇappan Abhaiyampukkāṉ Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ Kaḍakkaṉchōḻa
Gaṅgadēvan, (a resident) of Tiruchchūr. It is also mentioned that the lamps were to
be burnt out of the fines collected from the temple environs (tirumaḍaiviḷāgam,) and
the priests of the above temple were authorised to collect the fines and maintain the
above charity.

l.note: The two letters ka te engraved below.

inscription.head:
No. 536.
(A.R. No. 536 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the east and north wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Records that Tirunalluḻāṉ Tirunaṭṭapperumān Tiyāgamēgam, (a resident) of
Maduraivāyil in Pēṟūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu (a sub-division) of
Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam made a gift of one thousand four hundred and sixty
kuḻi of land in four different pieces, which he bought from the sabhaiyār of Kōvūr
in Māṅgāṭṭu-nāḍu and enjoyed as his kāṇi, to the god, Nīrvaṇṇapperumāḷ, as
tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam commencing from the month of Māśi. Among the boundaries of
the land mentioned, reference is made to a piece of land which this donor
previously donated to this temple as tirunāmattikkāṇi.

l.ta:
vatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu puliyūr
koṭṭamāṉa kulottuṅkacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu peṉūrnāṭṭu

maturai vāyiṟ[t] tirunalluḻāṉ tirunaṭṭapperumāṉ tiyākamekaṉeṉ
immaṇṭalattu ikkoṭṭattu māṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu kovūr capaiyār
pakkal eṉakku kāṇiyāka nāṉ vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya tiruṉīrmalai
nāyaṉār

nirvaṇṇapperumāḷ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam kovūr piṟinta iraṇṭāṅ
kaṭṭaḷaiyil teṉtuṇṭattil vaṭaviḷākattil paḻantirunāmattukkāṇi
nilam oru v[e]liyum nikki eṉ kāṇiyil iṉṉāyaṉār nīrvaṇṇap-

perumāḷukkut tirunāmattukkāṇiyāka nāṉviṭṭa cāṉakkālukku
vaṭakkil turavuvalam pulam kuḻi eṇṇūṟṟu nāṟpatum itaṉ teṉ
kiḻakku ikkāl cernta kuṇṭil

kuḻi nūṟṟeḻupatum itaṉ vaṭakkut toppil teṟkaṭaiya kuḻi
nūṟṟaimpatum itaṉ teṉkiḻakku tiruttil teṉkūṟu kuḻi muṉṉūṟum
āka kuḻi āyirattunāṉūṟṟuaṟupatum ikkuḻi āyirattu

nānūṟṟu aṟupatum ivvāṇṭai mācimātam mutal cantirātittavarai
celvatāka viṭṭeṉ tirunallūḻāṉ tirunaṭṭapperumāṉ
tiyākamekaṉeṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 537.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the north wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Jaṭāvarman Tribhuvanachchakkravarttigaḷ Śrī Sundarapāṇḍya (III)
Year 17, Mīna, śu. di. 5. Wednesday, Tiruvōṇam = 30th July 1292 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of six vēli of land at Tirunīrmalai and also the
assignment of taxes collected from the residents of temple quarters (tirumaḍaiviḷāgam)
of the Tirunīrmalai temple, as sarvamāṉya-iṟaiyili to the god Nāyaṉar Nīrvaṇṇapperumāḷ
of Tirunīrmalai in Śurattūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam, (a sub-division of) Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-
maṇḍalam by Aruṇagiriperumāḷ alias Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ.

l.ta:
ṇṭalattu puliyūrkkoṭṭattuc curattūrnāṭṭu tirunirmalaināyaṉār
nirvaṇṇapperumāḷukku aruṇa-

kiripperumāḷāṉa nilakaṅkarai[ya]neṉ iṉṉāyaṉār tiruvaṭivāram
tirunāmattukāṇi tirunirmalaiviḷākam tarappaṭi ni-

la[m*] āṟuveliyum tirumaṭaiviḷākat[til] puṟakkalaṉaik kuṭimakkaḷ
peril koḷḷumiṟaiyum patiṉeḻāvatu paṅkuṉi mātamu-

inscription.head:
No. 538.
(A.R. No. 538 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the north wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Jaṭāvarman Śrī Sundarapāṇḍya Year 11: 1286 A.D.

inscription.p:
Fragment and complete. Mentions the territorial division Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.
Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 539.
(A.R. No. 539 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the west and south walls, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

l.ta:
patāvatu tirunirmalaiemperumānukku malaiyampākkattu kai •••rāyaṇa
viḷakkan vaitta

l.note: At first the engraver wrote kocikabhaṭṭaṉ. ḷater he wrote aruḷāḷabhaṭṭaṉ over it.

inscription.head:
No. 540.
(A.R. No. 540 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkaravartti Vīrarājēndrachōḷa
Year 12: Mēsha, śu. di. 13 (mistake for śu. 7). Monday, Puṇarpūśam = 1256 A.D. April 3.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that a gift of a māḍai by uḍaiyāṉ Vaḍuganāthan
Mārippiḷḷai alias Arumārisādaṉ a resident of Pulippākkam alias Silachintāmaṇi-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam (a sub-division) of Āmūr-kōṭṭam for burning a twilight lamp to
the god (emberumāṉ) at Tirunīrmalai. It is also stated that Tirunīrmalainambi
Āṇḍabirāṉ Kēśava Bhaṭṭaṉ (a priest of the temple) received the gift and agreed to
burn the lamp perpetually.

l.ta: ḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattiṉāḷ tirunīrmalai emperumāṉukku āmuṟ ko-

ṭaiyāṉ vaṭukanātan mārippiḷḷaiyāṉa arumāritātaṉeṉ vaitta
cantiviḷakku

c cantiviḷakku oṉṟukkum ikkoyil naimicai tirunīrmalainampi
āṇṭapirāṉ kecava

inscription.head:
No. 541.
(A.R. No. 541 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Vīrarājēndrachōḷa Year: Pañchami, Sunday, uttiram.

inscription.p:
In characters of the 13th century A.D. This inscription records that a certain
Bhaṭṭaṉ Bhaṭṭāla Vēḷāṉ for burning a twilight lamp to the god of Tirunīrmalai
gifted one māḍai. The priests of the temple, Naimiśai. Bhaṭṭa Sōmāśi Śrī Vāśudēva
Bhaṭṭaṉ and Śrīdhara Bhaṭṭaṉ received the gift and agreed to burn the lamp
perpetually.

l.ta:
miyum nāyaṟṟuk kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirattunāḷ tirunīrmalai
nāyana ••••••••••

rk kiḻār kuṭi makkaḷil paṭṭaṉ paṭṭāla veḷāṉ vaitta canti
viḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 542.
(A.R. No. 542 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Vīrarājēndrachōḷa
Year 4, Makara, śu. di 10, Monday, Aviṭṭam = 1278 A.D. Other details do not match.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. This epigraph records that Aviravāṉṉi Perumāḷ Selvaṉ alias
Tiruvaraṅgadāṉaṉ, a merchant of Pūvirundavalli-nagaram made a gift of twilight
lamp to the god Nīrvaṇṇaṉ of Tirunīrmalai. Other details are lost.

l.ta:
m tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa aviṭṭattu nāḷ tirunirmalai nāyanār
nirvaṇṇaṉukku pūviruntamalli nakarattu vāṇiyaril avira

v[ā]ṉṉi perumāḷ celvanāṉa tiruvara[ṅkatāṉaṉ] vaitta canti
viḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 543.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartigaḷ Rājarājadēva III Year 6 Aippasi: 1222 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records that Naimisai Yagñanārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ, a priest in the at Tirunīrmalai,
had received 1(1/2) paḻaṅkāśu from Tirunaḍaṉampurivāṉ alias Uṟaṅgāvillidādan, a Malayāḷar
of Vaḷḷuva-nāḍu and undertook the responsibility of burning perpeturally a twilight
lamp in the above temple commencing from the month of Aippasi.

inscription.head:
No. 544.
(A.R. No. 544 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar perumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Rājarājadēva III Year 16, Dhanus, śu. di. 5. Sunday, Aviṭṭam = November 30, 1231 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of 1(3/4) paḻaṅkāśu by Aruḷāḷap-Perumāḷ Śīyaṉ (a resident) of
Peruṅgaṉalvaṭṭam, for burning a twilight lamp to the god at Tirunīrmalai. The priest
of the temple, Āsūri Malaikiṉiya-niṉṟāṉ Bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja gōtra, received the
gift and agreed to maintain the lamp perpetually.

inscription.head:
No. 545.
(A.R. No. 545 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganāthaperumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Vīrarājēndra Year 4 Mīna, śu. di. 12. Sunday = 8th March 1248 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of 2 māḍai by an individual, Araśāṇḍai, a resident of Pūṇḍi
in order to burn two twilight lamps to the god Nīrvaṇṇan Tirunīrmalai-nāyanār
temple. The temple priest Naimisai Yagñanārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭ[aṉ] received the gift and
agreed to burn the lamp perpetually.

inscription.head:
No. 546.
(A.R. No. 546 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇaperumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartigaḷ Śrī Vīrarājēndrachōḷa
Year 34: Mēsha, śu. di. 5 Mrigaśirsba, Wednesday = March 30, 1278 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of 10 paṇam by Kaliyugamēyaṉ alias Nandipaṉmaṉ, one of the
agambaḍmudali of Sōḻagaṅgadēva in order to burn a twilight lamp to the deity
Nīrvaṇṇaperumāḷ at Tirunīrmalai in Śurattūr-nāḍu of Kulōttuṅgasōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in
Puliyūr-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. Naimiśai Kauśika
Bhaṭṭaṉ, the priest of the temple had received the gift and agreed to burn the lamp
perpetually.

inscription.head:
No. 547.
(A.R. No. 547 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇaperumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Vijayagaṇḍagōpala Year 24, Vṛichchika, ba. di. 5,
Wednesday, Pūsam = 1 November, 1273 A.D.

inscription.p:
Incomplete. Beginning lines are wrongly engraved. This inscription registers a
gift of a māḍai by a lady Perumāṇāch-chiṅgaḷa aṇukkiyār of Bammal in order to
burn a twilight lamp to the god Nīrvaṇṇapperumāḷ of the temple at Tirunīrmalai in
Śuṟattūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 548.
(A.R. No. 548 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇa-perumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Kulōttuṅga III Year 10; Makra, śu. di. 5 Tiruvōṇam, Monday, 1188 A.D.

inscription.p:
This record is faintly engraved, hence certain letters could not be made out
satisfactorily. It registers a gift of a coin Gaṇḍagōpālan-pudumāḍai by Tillai-nāyagaṉ,
a resident of Nelmali in Paḍuvūr-nāḍu in Āmūr-kōṭṭam for burning a twilight lamp
to the god Nīrvaṇṇaṉ of the temple at Tirunīrmalai in Śurattu-nāḍu in Puliryūr-
kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. The priest of the temple,
Nārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ, son of Śrī Kṛishṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ received the gift and agreed to burn
the lamp perpetually.

No. 549.
(A.R. No. 549 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇaperumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Rājarāja III Year •••Dhanu, Purva, Avittam Sunday.

inscription.p:
It registers a gift of 1(3/4) paḻaṅkāśu by Irāppaḍaiveṉṟāṉ, one of the agambaḍiyār
Piḷḷaiyār Sōḻagaṅgadēvar to burn a twilight lamp to the god at Tirunīrmalai. A priest
of the temple, Yagñanārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ received the gift and agreed to burn the
lamp.

No. 550.
(A.R. No. 550 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the Nīrvaṇṇar perumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ
temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Rājarājadēva (Rājarāja III)
Year 14, Rishabha, Attam, śu. di. 10. Wednesday = 22nd May 1230 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of 1(3/4) paḻaṅkāśu by Paṭṭāladēvaṉ Paḷḷikoṇḍāṉ (a native) of
Peruṅganalvaṭṭam in order to burn a twilight lamp to the god at Tirunīrmalai. The
temple priest Naimisai Kṛishṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ received the gift and agreed to maintain this
twilight lamp perpetually.

inscription.head:
No. 551.
(A.R. No. 551 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall of the Nīrvaṇṇar perumāḷ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ
temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Vīrarājēndra (Rājarāja III)
Year 2; Siṁha, śu. di. 2. Attam, Friday = 25th August 1245 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of 32 cows for burning a perpetual lamp to the god Nīrvaṇṇaṉ
of the temple at Tirunīrmalai by four persons, Śārumbam Nandi Pallavaraiyaṉ,
Kambakkōṉ Bhaṭṭaṉ alias Nīlagaṅgaraiyakkōṉ, Eṭṭisipākkadātan and another person
(name damaged). Two shepherds, Tiruvagattikkōṉ, (a resident) of Irumbuliyūr and
Aṇṇāmalai Añjal alias Perumāḷ, received 16 cows each and had agreed to measure
out 1/4 āḻākku and 1 uḻakku of oil daily for burning the perpetual lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 552.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravarttigaḷ Śrī Vīrarājēndrachōḻa
Year: 6, Tula. śu. di. 13. Rēvathi, Wednesday = 21st October 1249 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of a Gaṇḍagōpalaṉ-pudumāḍai by a certain Kō-Viḻupparaiyaṉ
Vīra-Chōḻaṉ, a native of Nandaṇambākkam, in order to burn a twilight lamp to the
god at Tirunīrmalai at Surattūr-nāḍu in Kulottuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu alias Puliyūr-kōṭṭam,
(a subdivision) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam. Naimisai Pāṇḍavadūta-Bhaṭṭar Śrīdhara
Bhaṭṭaṉ, a priest of the temple received the gift and agreed to maintain the lamp
perpetually.

inscription.head:
No. 553.
(A.R. No. 553 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription contains a lot of mistakes, presumably engraved by an ignorant
mason. In several places it is difficult to make out any meaningful reading.

In the Annual report of 1911-12 it is stated that the grant portion of this record
commencing from lines 15-24 is formed a part of the No. 539. But a close
examination of this portion reveals that it has nothing to do with the above
inscription. The style of writing as well as the orthographical mistakes which the
engraver committed in both the records i.e., the present record and the lines 15-24
attributed to A.R.No. 539 tempt us to connect these ten lines with the present one
rather than with No. 539.
Begins with the meykīrtti of the ruler, Pūmālai miḍaṁdu etc;

It records the sale of land probably by a certain Kuḷattuḻāṉ Peruṅkāḍaṉ
Vīravallān, with all its appurtenances and irrigation facilities, to the god of Tirunīrmalai.
It is stated that the land was also freed from taxes such as antarāyam, nīrvilai,
echchōṟu, kūṟṟunel etc.

Several persons, Veṇkāḍaṉ Sēndaṉ of Māndūr, Peruṅkāḍaṉ Villi of Kuḷattur
and others figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 554.
(A.R. No. 554 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the north wall of the Chakravarti-Tirumagaṉ shrine,
Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Tribhuvanachchakkravarttigaḷ Sundara Pāṇḍya Year 16: 1293 A.D.

inscription.p:
Ends of the lines are built in. This inscription is in the 12-13th century
characters. It records an agreement among three parties i.e., 1) A merchant from
Pammal, Kuṉṟam-uḍaiyāṉ Meiñānavittakan Tiruveṇkāḍu-uḍaiyāṉ, 2) the trustees
(taṉattār) of the Nakkar nāyanār temple, 3) prince (piḷḷaiyār). Accordingly, both the
merchant and the tāṉattār agreed to conduct several rituals and services i.e., the
procession of the deity, perform offerings and worship in the temple in the months
Paṅguni and Vaikāśi, supply various commodities for providing food offerings and
articles for adorning both the god and goddess, out of the endowment, probably
created by the prince.

l.ta:
vutu uṭaiyā[r*] pammaṉakkar nāyaṉār koyil(ta) tāṉattāṟku
pammali irukkum viyāpārikaḷilk kuṉṟamuṭaiyāṉ meruñāṉa
vittakaṉ tiruveṇkāṭuṭaiyāṉe tiruve ••••••••••

tirupavaṉikku tiruppaṅkuṉit tirunāḷukkum tiruvaikācit tirunāḷukku[m]
melum kaṇun tirunāḷkaḷukkum tiruppavaṉikkuṟaivaṟuppukkup
piḷḷaiyār tirumuṉpākat tāṉattāruṭaṉuṅkūṭa niccayittu
nāṉupaiya[ṅ*]koṇṭapaṭi ••••••••••

kku arici tūṇippatakkum kaṟiamutukku vāḻaikkāy aimpatum
vaḻutalaikkāy aimpatum teṅkāy oṉṟum vāḻai[p*]paḻam iru
l.note: The letter iru is found written above the line.

pattum
karuppukkaṭṭi vaṭṭu irupatum miḷaku amutu uḻakkum uppu amutu
nāḻiyum taiyi ••••••••••

tirumālai oraṇaiyum tiruneṟṟimālai oṉṟum tiruvirakkaḻal oṉṟum
tiruttaṭāpārattukku veḷḷaittoṭaiyum nācciyārkku veḷḷait
tirumālai oraṇaiyum ti[ru] ••••••••••

araikkaḻaiñcum aṭaikkāyamutu pākku irupatu veṟṟilai nāṟpatu
cipātantāṅkuvārkkup paṇam iraṇṭum tiruvi ••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 555.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the west wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Māṟavarman Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Kulaśēkhara
Year 37: Kaṟkaṭaka, śu. di. 5, Wednesday, uttiram = 8th July 1304 A.D.

inscription.p:
This incomplete inscription records the assignment of the village Vaḍakkuppaṭṭu
with all its appurtenances, after changing its name, Kumāragōpālanallūr, after his
name (nam pērāle) to the god Nakka-nāyanar, of the temple at Pammal in Surattūr-
nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-
maṇḍalam by Pañchanadivāṇaṉ Tiruvēgambaṉ alias Nīlagaṅgaṉ. It is stated that the
latter assigned this village to the god for celebrating the day of his natal star Hasta
in the month of Paṅguni. It is further mentioned that the ceded village was
purchased, from the people (who were called) Neḍuṅkaṇṇagaḷ and Vaḷḷaikāppugaḷ,
who resided at Perumpākkam alias Rājarājanallūr in Kāvāy-nāḍu by a certain
Tirukkāḷatti-uḍaiyāṉ alias Narasiṅgapaṉmaṉ, one of the agampaḍiyās of Kūttampākkam,
who in turn made over to the donor Pañchanadivāṇaṉ.

l.ta:
pakṣattu
pañcamiyum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirattu(ṉa)nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattup puliyūrkoṭṭamāṉa kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu

nilakaṅkaṉeṉ uṭaiyār pammanakkanāyaṉārkku tiruppaṅkuṉi mātam nam
jaṉmanakṣa

inscription.head:
No. 556.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the west wall, Chakravartti-Tirumagaṉ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ
temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartigaḷ Vīrarājēndrachōḻa
Year 34, Ṛishabha, śu. di. 3, Wednesday, Puṇarpūśam = 25th May 1278 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged and incomplete. It seems to record the assignment of taxes such as
poṉvari, nāṭṭuvari, kudimagaṇmai, kāṇikkaipēruk-kāśu and other taxes collected from
the temple environs (tirumaḍaiviḷāgam) by (the official) Pañchanidivāṇaṉ Nallanāyaṉ
alias Chōḻagaṅgadēvaṉ for conducting offerings and worship to the god Nakkanāyaṉār
of Pammal on the auspicious occasion (in the month) of Tiruvaikāśi.

l.ta:
[ca]ttu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattup puliyūrkoṭṭamāṉa
kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu curattūrnāṭṭu pammalil [u]ṭaiyār
pamlala

[ni]tivāṇaṉ nallanāyaṉāṉa coḻakaṅkatevaneṉ uṭaiyār pammaṉakka
nāyaṉāṟku tiruvai[y*]kāci tirunāḷaikku nām i •••••••••• vāka
iṉṉāyaṉār tirumaṭ[ai]-

viḷākattu niyāyattār perilk kaṭaimai poṉvari nāṭṭuvari
kuṭimakaṇmai kāṇikkai peṟṟukkācu paṭṭikuṟṟam

edition.l: paṭuvari kuṭimakaṉmai Read makaṇmai••••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 557.
(A.R. No. 557 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Chakravartti-Tirumagaṉ shrine, same temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravarttin Vīrarājēndrachōḻa 1277 A.D.
Year 33, Kaṟkaṭaka, śu. di. 10. Wednesday, Uttiram, 17th July 1210 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the (assignment) of ten vēli of land, as dēvadāna by
(the chief) Pañchanadivāṇa Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ Nallanāyaṉ alias Chōḻagaṅgadēvaṉ at
Aṇaikkaraichchēri alias Chōḻagaṅgadēvanallūr to the god Nakkanāyanār of (the
village) Pammal. It is stated that the above village Aṇaikkaraichchēri, (wherein the
gifted land was situated) was obtained as kāṇi by a certain Vaḷattuvāḻvittār, son of
Tiruvaṇṇāmalai-uḍaiyāṉ alias Ayyanaṅgāṟappiramarāyar, who is also described as one
of the Āriyars, from the sabhaiyār of Nallūr alias Chōḻanaṉbach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Mēṉmāṅgāṭṭu-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.

Evidently the chief Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ made this gift of land after purchase from
Vaḷattuvāḻvittār, though this is not explicitly stated in the record.

l.ta:
[mi]yum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirattu(ṉa)nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa
coḻamaṇṭalattup puliyūrkoṭṭattu meṉmāṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu • •
• • nallūrāṉa coḻanaṉpaccatu

yanār coḻakaṅka te ••••• sa • nilam pattu velikku kiḻpārkkellai

ūrnat[ta]-

ttukku teṟkkum āka [iṉṉā]ṉkellaiyuḷ naṭu uḷpaṭṭa mikutik
kuṟaivu uḷḷaṭaṅka nilam pattuveliyum tevatāṉamāka iṭṭom
pañcanativāṇa nilakaṅkaraiyaṉ nallanāyanāṉa coḻakaṅka tevaṉeṉ

§ śrīmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 558.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Chakravartti-Tirumagaṉ shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ
temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravarttin Vīrarājēndrachōḻa
Year 34, Rishabha, śu. di. 3, Puṇar pūśam = 25th May 1278 A.D.

inscription.p: Fragment. Contains only the date portion. Other details are lost.

l.ta: tiyaiyum putaṉkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa puṇarpūcattu nāḷ jeyaṅ

inscription.head:
No. 559.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the two detached slabs fixed into the main gōpura, Raṅganātha perumāḷ
temple.
inscription.p:
Built in. Seems to record the assignment of taxes like kaḍamai, āyam, kāśukaḍamai
paṭṭikuṟṟam, and other taxes accrued on three pieces of land (located at) Taṇṇiyālattūr,
Arumbaḷḷi and Puḻichchatūr by the chief Pañchanadivāṇa Nīlagaṅgarayaṉ for a
temple caused to be built by a certain Mudaliyār at (a place) Paḷḷipaḍai-agaram in
Taṇṇiyālāttur in Surattūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam.
This inscription is in characters of the 12th century A.D.

l.ta:
•••••••••• coḻamaṇṭalattu puliyūrkoṭṭamāṉa
kulottuṅkacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu curattūrnāṭṭu maṭappuṟam
taṇṇiyālattūril mutaliyār paḷḷipaṭaiakarattu eḻuntaru-

•••••••••• kkum uṭalāka nām viṭṭa ittaṇṇiyālattūril

ikkoṭṭattu innāṭṭu arumpaḷḷiyil taṭi uḷvarippaṭi viṭṭa
nilam oru ve-

paṭṭil nammu[ṭ]aiya pūntoṭṭaṅkaḷilt tiruṉantavaṉamāka viṭṭa
āṟṟaṅkarai kollai oḻukiṉpaṭi kuḻi eṇṇūṟṟiṉāl nilam eṭṭu
māvum āka ni-

•••••••••• ṅkakum iṉṉāḷ mutal arimapāṭikāvalil ūrkāviṟ
• pura koḷḷum nālil oṉṟu nikki allāta ṉām koḷḷum kaṭamai
āyam kācu kaṭamai paṭṭikuṟṟam kāttikaippaccai

inscription.head:
No. 560.
(A.R. No. 560 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the west wall, Narasiṁhasvāmin shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartti Vīrarājēndrachōḻa
Year 3: Siṁha, śu. di. 10, Monday, Aviṭṭam = 30th July 1246 A.D. but the tithi was 15 not 10.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that Ayiravaṇṇan Āḷkoṇḍavilli alias Tirukkachchi
Nambidāsaṉ, a merchant of the nagaram Pūvirundavalli, gifted one twilight lamp to
the god Siṅgapperumāḷ (of the temple) at Tirunīrmalai. A priest of the temple
Nayimisai Yagñanārāyaṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ Āyappiḷḷai had received one Gaṇḍagōpalan-māḍai
and agreed to burn the lamp.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirarājendracoḻatevaṟku
yāṇṭu 3- vatu simhanāyaṟṟu pū-

l.ta:
miyum tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa aviṭṭattu nāḷ
tirunirmalaināyanār ciṅkapperumāḷukku

pūviruntamallinakarattu vāṇiyaril ayiravaṇṇan āḷkoṇṭa
villiyāṉa tirukkaccinampi

iccantiviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyil nampimāril
nayimicai yajñanārāyaṇa

inscription.head:
No. 561.
(A.R. No. 561 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Nīrvaṇṇar shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Rājarāja III Year 23: Mithuna, ba. di. 10. Sunday, Aśvati, 29th May, 1239 A.D. f.d.n. 6.

inscription.p:
Records a gift of a Gaṇḍagōpalaṉpudumāḍai by a certain Pākkiḻāṉ Bhaṭṭālagaṉ
of Peruṅgaṉalvaṭṭam to burn a twilight lamp to the god, Śiṅgapperumāḷ, the god of
Tirunīrmalai.

l.ta:
tināḷ tirunirmalai nāyanār ciṅkapperumāḷukkup
peruṅkaṉalvaṭṭam pākkiḻāṉ paṭṭālakaṉ

kkoṇṭa [kaṇ*ṭa]kopalaṉ
putu māṭai+kaikkoṇṭu [i]kkoyilkkāṇi uṭaiya naiyimicai
vāḻava-

inscription.head:
No. 562.
(A.R. No. 562 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Narasiṁhasvāmin shrine, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartigaḷ śrī Rājarājadēva (III)
Year 19, Mēsha, śu. di. 12, Tiruvōṇam, Monday = 2nd April 1235 A.D.

inscription.p:
Records a gift one Gaṇḍagōpalaṉ-māḍai by Gōvinda Bhaṭṭān, a resident of
Niṉṟavūr in Puḻar-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, for burning a twilight
lamp to the god Siṅgaperumāḷ of Nīrvaṇṇan temple. Two priests, Vāradarāja
Bhaṭṭaṉ, and Siṅgapperumāḷ, sons of Naimisai Śrī Kṛishṇa Bhaṭṭa Sōmayāji, who
possessed kāṇi right, in the temple received the gift and agreed to maintain the said
twilight lamp perpetually.

l.ta: ṭṭur ṉāya puliyūrkoṭṭattu [kā]ṇi[y] pūtūr eḻuti uyaiya

inscription.head:
No. 563.
(A.R. No. 563 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Narasiṁhasvāmin shrine, same temple.

p.b:
King: Tribhuvanachchakkravartigaḷ Rāja[rāja]dēva III
Year 14: Rishabha, śu. di. 10, Thursday, Hastam = 23 May 1230 A.D.

inscription.p:
It records a gift of 1(3/4) paḻaṅkāśu by a native of (village), Peruṅgaṉalvaṭṭam for
burning a twilight lamp to the god at Tirunīrmalai. The priest of the temple,
Śrī Kṛishṇa Bhaṭṭan received the gift and agreed to maintain the lamp perpetually.

inscription.head:
No. 564.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the north wall, Yāgamaṇḍapa, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.

p.b: King: Vīraveṅkaṭarāya. Year Śārvari, Vaikāśi 20, 1600-01 A.D.

inscription.p:
Damaged. This inscription is very carelessly engraved. It seems that during the
headship (pārāpattiyam) of a certain Tāttāchāriya some gift was made to the
Aḻagiyasiṅga (shrine) of Tirunīrmalai. Other details are not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 565.
(A.R. No. 565 of 1912.)
TIRUNĪRMALAI, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On the south wall, Raṅganātha perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p:
Much damaged. Refers to a certain Tattāchariyar. Other details lost. In characters
of the 15th century A.D.

edition.l: • • muta ••••• daya ••••

inscription.head:
No. 566.
(A.R. No. 566 of 1912.)
MAHĀBALIPURAM, CHINGLEPET TALUK, CHINGLEPET DISTRICT.
On a plinth of two balipīṭhas in the western side, second courtyard, shore
temple.
inscription.p: Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XIX. pp. 105 ff.

inscription.head:
No. 567.
(A.R. No. 567 of 1912.)
TIRUMALA, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
On a tier of the south wall, central shrine, Śrīnivāśa-Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Published in Tirumala-Tirupati Dēvasthnam Inscriptions Vol. IV. No. 13, pp 27-28.

inscription.head:
No. 568.
(A.R. No. 568 of 1912.)
TIRUMALA, CHANDRAGIRI TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
On the 150 copper-plates preserved in the Taḷḷapākaṁ-koṭṭu within the first
circuit of the Śrīnivāśa-Perumāḷ temple.
inscription.p: Published in Annamayya Saṇkūtanalu by T.T.D.

p.b:
King: Achyutarāya Year: Śaka 1459, Hemavaḷambi,
Phalguṇa ba. di. 7, Guruvāra = Thursday, 21 February 1538 A.D.

inscription.p:
This records the gift of a canal consisted of a talaparigi channel below the
tank of Nagaragere and of a tank called Gūḷiseṭikuṇṭe in Aḍabāṟahaḷḷi, the water
of which was to be used for the sacred bath (abhishēka) of the gods Pāpavināśadēva,
Raghunāthadēva and Vīrēśvaradēva of Lēpākshi. Also it records a grant of land for
providing food offerings (amṛitapaḍi) to these three gods. The donors were the
aśēsha-mahājanas of Nagaragere alias Kṛishṇarāya-samudra, belonging to Rodanāḍ
in Penugoṇḍe-rājya. The recipient was Viraṇṇa, younger brother of Virupaṇṇa of
Penugoṇḍa.

It is also stated that this record was a copy of the original letter-charter
(Patra-śāsahr).

p.b:
King: Achyutarāya Year Śaka 1456, Jaya, Vaiśākha, ba. di. 15,
Mīna-saṅkramaṇa = 28th April Thursday 1534 A.D.

inscription.p:
The first portion is in Sanskrit and it refers to a certain Vīra-Bhūpala, younger
brother of Vīrapaṇṇa and son of Muddāmbika and Nandi-Lakēndra. It is said that
it was he who made the grant. The second potion records that all the sēṭṭi-
paṭṭaṇasvāmis including Ayyōvoḷe of ubhaya-nānādēśa made a grant of revenue
yielding from certain taxes levied on weekly Sunday fairs at Vīrēśvarapura in the
village Lēpākshi of Roda-nāḍ in Penngoṇḍa rājya. The gift was meant for the
services like incense, lamp, amṛitapaḍi, dance, music-both vocal and instrumental
-to the gods Pāpavināśa-dēva, Vīrēśvara and Raghunātha of Lēpākshi.

inscription.head:
No. 571.
(A.R. No. 571 of 1912.)
LĒPĀKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On the east wall, second prākāra, Vīrabhadra temple.

p.b:
King: Achyutarāya Year: Śaka 1459, Hemaḷambi, Jyeshṭha, ba. di. 10,
Sōmavāra = 2nd June 1537 A.D. However, the week day was Saturday.

inscription.p:
This registers a sale-deed of land made by the mahājanas of Kañchasamudra
alias Pratāpa Dēvarāyapura to the gods Pāpavināśadēva, Vīrēśvara and Raghunātha
of Lēpākshi. During the reign-period of Dēvarāya the village Kañchasamudra,
renamed as Pratāpa Dēvarāyapura was made a sarvamānya-agrahāra. The record
refers to three great gifts made by him. The gifted village was situated near
Lēpākshi of Sādali-vēṇṭe in Roda-nāḍu of Penugoṇḍe-rājya.
The specified lands were sold for one hundred gaṭṭi-varaha.

l.ka:

p.b:
King: Achyutarāya Year: Śaka 1459, Hemaḷambi, Kārttīka, ba. di. 12,
(utthāna-dvādaśi). Monday = 15th October 1537 A.D.

inscription.p:
The latter portion of the record is damaged and the details are not clear.
However, it registers a grant of the village Kāḷepalli renamed as Achyutēndra-
mahārāyapura, for the services of lord Vīrēśvara of Lēpākshi. The donor was the son
of Nandi Lakki-seṭṭi and Muddamāmbā. The stone charter was, it is stated, a copy
of the copper-plate record.

inscription.head:
No. 573.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1912.)
LĒPĀKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On the east wall, second prākāra, Vīrabhadra temple.
inscription.p:
This inscription which belongs to the period of the Vijayanagar and it records
the gift of villages to the temples. It also mentions that this grant was engraved an
stone by Basava-Bhaṭṭa, Pādhyāra and the chief accountant (Sēnabāva)-Gaṇakāgarēśara,
Timmap[p]a.

l.ka:
śubhamastu^1^ | lēpākṣi vīrabhadrasya | pāpanāśēśvarasya ca | śrīrāmasya ca saṁbaṁdhi |
grāmasīmā

pādhyāya ya-

saṁskṛta ślōka vū¨rvakaḥ |

sanagaḷige maṁgaḷamahā śrī śrī śrī jēyanu ||

p.b: King: Achyutadēva Year: Śaka 1455, ijaya, Kārttika, Utthānadvādaśi = October 29, 1533 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription gives the titles of the ruling sovereign Achyutadēva. It records
that the king made a gift of the villages Chōḷasamudra in the Penukoṇḍarājya,
Hampaṇṇapalli and Kañchalarahaḷḷi, after renaming the latter village as Veṇkaṭādri-
rāya-Samudra, for the merit of his son Veṇkaṭādri-rāya, to the temples at Lēpakshi
i.e., Pāpanāsa-liṅga, Ramapati and Vīrabhādra.

l.ka:
śrī pāpanāśaliṁgasya śrīrāmasyaramāpatēḥ | śrī vīrabhadradēvasya śrīpādakamalaṁ
bhajē | lēpākṣi vīrabhadrēṇa bhujyamānāsadāmudā | kumāra śrīveṁkaṭādri •

kavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1455 neya vijaya saṁvatsarada kā¨rtika śu 12 sa vuthāna dvādaśi puṁṇya
kāladalū tuṁgabhadrātīra śrīviṭhalēśvara dēvara saṁnitiyalū śrī

tapuva rāyaragaṁḍa | hiṁdurāyasuratrāṇa śrīvīrapratāpa śrī·a

[cyu]tadēva mahārāyaru taṁma kumāra veṁkaṭādrirāyara hesaralū lēpākṣi
naṁdilakiseṭe mudamāṁbāvara kumāra penugoṁḍa vīrapaṁṇana[va]rige nitya
paḍi nayivēdya mānitavāda vayibhavaṁgaḷige penugoṁḍerājya cōḷasamudra

inscription.head:
No. 575.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1912.)
LĒPĀKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On the eastern wall, second prākāra, Vīrabhadra temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX. Part II No: 572.

inscription.head:
No. 576.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1912.)
LĒPĀKSHI, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On the east wall, second prākāra, Vīrabhadra temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX. Part II No: 580.

p.b:
King: ⎯⎯ Year: Śaka 1603, Durmati, Vaiśākha, śu. di. 3 Monday,
solar eclipse = 9th, May 1681, but the day was Tuesday.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of tolls by the merchants of all countries
headed by Pṛithiviseṭṭi Rāyana-Bhāskara, to the temple of Sānta Naṅjuṁḍēśvara at
Lēpākshi.

l.te: śrī śāṁta naṁjuṁḍēśvarāyanamaḥ svasti śrī vijayābhyudaya śālivāhana-
ṁḍēśvarasvāmi sthānika·ārādhyalakuhattiyyaku roddanāḍu penugoṁḍa svasti samasta
nija sāmahaṁtāḷi praśasti sahitulaina śrīmadgaṇēśvara gaurīśvara dēvvvara(dēva)

śrīpādapadmārādhakulaina svadēśa paradēśa vubhaya nānādēśa vayyāvaḷi mukhya
śrīmanmahā • • • • •

p.b:
King: Hajarata Sultāna Abdula Hasanu Alaṁgi[ru] Pannāha (Abdul Hasan Qutb Shah)
Year: Śaka 1602, Raudri, Vaiśākha, śu. di. 3 Monday = 21 April 1680 A.D. But the day was Wednesday.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of fields, gardens and coconut groves to the
temple of Śānta Naṁjuṇḍēśvara for providing sacred water (abhishēkam), oil lamps
and other offerings (nivedya) to the god for the merit of Hazarat Sultan by the
Reḍḍis and Karaṇams of (old) Lēpākshi-sthaḷa.

l.te: śrī śāṁtanaṁjuṁḍēśvarasvāmi svastiśrī vijayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁ-
bulu 1602 ·agunēṭi ravudrināma saṁvatsaraṁ vayiśākha śu 3 sōmavāraṁ-
(gā) hajarat yelina vāriki sukṛtaṁgānu prā • lēpākṣistaḷaṁ
sthānaṁ kiṁdi kōnēraṁcu maḍilō 2 yiddumu dēvaḷanaku vutta-
raṁ nāgalakaṭṭa kiṁda baṁḍaṁbu maḍimēla 2 ·iddumu vubhayaṁ ma-
ḍi nalutumu tōṭalu dēvaḷānaku paḍamaramallaṁbhaṭla maḍikiṁda

inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX. Part II No. 631.

CHAḶŪR, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On a stone set up near the Āñjanēya temple.

p.b: King: ⎯⎯ Year: Śaka 1471, Sowmya, Niyavaiśākha, ba. di. 6. Sukra-vāra, 17th May Friday 1549 A.D.

inscription.p:
The pictures of moon, sun, disc and counch are depicted. It records the
construction of the temple for the god Hanumantadēva (Āñjanēya) and a maṇḍapa
in front of the garbha-guḍi.

l.ka: mya saṁvatsarada nijavaiśākha ba 6 śukradalū śrīmatu
svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt śrī śrī śrī

edition.p: [On the top, portion of the stone-much damaged.]

p.b: King: Eregaṅga Permānaḍi Year: Śaka 775 (853 A.D.)

inscription.p:
Fragmentary. This Kannada inscription states that Eregaṅga Permānaḍi was
ruling over a territory up to Kāñchīpuram. Also refers to a certain Budda, who
was ruling the division (name lost).

In characters of 18-19th century A.D. This Kannada inscription records a tax-free
grant of land, under the tank of the village Ūṭukūru, to the Besta-bova (fishermen
community) for attending their traditional work connected with the tank, by the
officers Mummaḍi-Raḍḍi, the agent of Pedirājayya and the gauḍas and sēnabova.

inscription.head:
No. 590.
(A.R. No. 590 of 1912.)
KOTNŪRU, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On a stone set up infront of the Chauḍēśvari temple.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX. Part II No: 639.

This inscription is in the characters of the 8-9th century A.D. It records a grant
of bittulaṭṭa (bittuvaṭṭi) land, for the maintenance of tanks, by the king. It contains
the names of gauṇḍas and ends with the usual imprecatory verses.

l.note: Lines 13 & 14 are written under the word‘vāraṇāsiyumaṁ’ of line 12.
Lines 13 & 14 are written under the‘vāraṇāsiyumaṁ’ of line 12.

No. 592.
(A.R. No. 592 of 1912.)
MANEPALLE, HINDUPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
On a stone lying near the Īśvara temple in the bed of the tank.
inscription.p: Published in S.I.I. Vol. IX. Part I No: 21.

Erased. This inscription on the grounds of palaeography may be assigned to the
7-8th century A.D. It records the destruction of the village Kottanūr, when a
goldsmith Kūrmāchāri and his father fought and perhaps got killed.

This stone contains the images (perhaps of the heroes) which are said to have
been sculpted by a certain Dhanapatiāchāri.
